Words: Rosa...我需要再核实一下你的陈述。事发当晚，你说你在哪里？
Rosa... I need to verify your statement again. The night of the incident, where did you say you were?
我...我告诉过你，我在打扫地下室走廊。从九点半开始，一直到...到很晚...
I... I told you, I was cleaning the basement corridor. Started at nine-thirty, until... until very late...
你的工作记录卡写得很清楚：11月4日，23:00到01:00，一楼走廊清洁。不是地下室。
Your work record card says it clearly: November 4th, 23:00 to 01:00, first floor corridor cleaning. Not the basement.
我...呃...我记错了...是的，一楼走廊...不是地下室...
I... um... I got it wrong... yes, the first floor corridor... not the basement...
一楼走廊。离接待室很近。你看到了什么？
The first floor corridor. Close to the reception room. What did you see?
什么都没看到！我只是在打扫...低着头擦地板...什么都没注意到...
Didn't see anything! I was just cleaning... head down scrubbing the floor... didn't notice anything...
你的储物间里有一条毛巾。闻起来像氯仿。
There's a towel in your storage room. It smells like chloroform.
那...那不是我的...我不知道那是什么...
That... that's not mine... I don't know what that is...
走廊垃圾桶里还有一个氯仿瓶，瓶口还是湿的。Rosa，你用氯仿把我迷晕了。
There's also a chloroform bottle in the corridor trash bin, still wet at the opening. Rosa, you knocked me out with chloroform.
...是的。是我。我用毛巾捂住了你的口鼻...对不起...真的对不起...
...Yes. It was me. I used the towel to cover your mouth and nose... I'm sorry... I'm so sorry...
...为什么？
... Why?
我女儿...她病了...病得很重...我没有钱...他说如果我帮忙，他就能帮我女儿...
My daughter... she's sick... very sick... I have no money... he said if I helped, he could help my daughter...
... Who?
是我...我一个人做的...我把你迷晕然后拖到接待室...我杀了Webb先生...
It was me... I did it alone... I knocked you out then dragged you to the reception room... I killed Mr. Webb...
走廊地板上的拖拽痕迹很深，至少有150磅的重量。Rosa，你体重不到120磅。你不可能拖动我。
The drag marks on the corridor floor are deep, at least 150 pounds. Rosa, you weigh less than 120 pounds. You couldn't have dragged me.
我...我用尽了全力...我...
I... I used all my strength... I...
你在替谁顶罪？
Who are you taking the fall for?
...我不能说...他会杀了我女儿...
...I can't say... he'll kill my daughter...
Rosa。我不是警察。我是来找真相的。你女儿需要一个能照顾她的母亲，不是一个替别人坐牢的母亲。
Rosa. I'm not the police. I'm here for the truth. Your daughter needs a mother who can take care of her, not a mother doing someone else's time in prison.
那你能保证不告诉别人吗？
Can you promise not to tell anyone?
我保证。
I promise.
...是Morrison...Morrison警官。他...他让我帮忙把你迷晕...说他只是想问你几个问题...我不知道他会...会杀人...
...It's Morrison... Officer Morrison. He... he asked me to help knock you out... said he just wanted to ask you some questions... I didn't know he would... would kill someone...
他是怎么威胁你的？
How did he threaten you?
他知道我女儿病了...说他认识医院的人...说他能让她免费治疗...但如果我不帮忙...他就会向移民局举报我...
He knew my daughter was sick... said he knew people at the hospital... said he could get her free treatment... but if I didn't help... he'd call immigration on me...
你把我迷晕之后发生了什么？
What happened after you knocked me out?
Morrison来了...他把你拖进了接待室...我太害怕了，躲在储物间里不敢出来...
Morrison came... he dragged you into the reception room... I was so scared, I hid in the storage room and didn't dare come out...
谢谢你告诉我这些，Rosa。照顾好你的女儿。
Thank you for telling me this, Rosa. Take care of your daughter.
Brennan先生...你...你不会告诉Morrison我说了这些，对吧...求你了...
Mr. Brennan... you... you won't tell Morrison I talked, will you... please...
我会处理Morrison的。你不用担心任何事。
I'll handle Morrison. You don't need to worry about anything.
Morrison。
Morrison.
Brennan。我办公室不欢迎嫌疑人。
Brennan. Suspects are not welcome in my office.
那我不会待太久。
Then I won't stay long.
我的报告写得很清楚。你不识字？
My report is clear. Can't you read?
我读过。写得不错。很专业，很详细，逻辑清晰，时间线完整——唯一的问题是，每一个关键细节都是精心设计的谎言。
I've read it. Well written. Professional, detailed, clear logic, complete timeline—the only problem is, every key detail is a carefully designed lie.
小心你的措辞，Brennan。诽谤警官可是重罪。现在，给我滚出去。
Watch your words, Brennan. Slandering an officer is a felony. Now, get out.
那我们就让证据说话。从你的时间线开始。
Then let's let the evidence speak. Starting with your timeline.
时间线？你他妈在我办公室里审问我？Brennan，我警告你，如果你不立刻滚出去，我会以妨碍公务罪逮捕你。
Timeline? Are you fucking interrogating me in my office? Brennan, I warn you, if you don't get out right now, I'll arrest you for obstruction of justice.
你可以试试。但那样的话，我就不得不把这些证据交给你的上司了。顺便说一句，我在Kirkland的时候，最擅长的就是整理证据链。
You can try. But then, I'd have to hand this evidence to your superior. By the way, at Kirkland, my specialty was organizing chains of evidence.
证据？你有什么证据？
Evidence? What evidence do you have?
很多。我们先聊聊你那晚的行踪。
A lot. Let's first talk about your whereabouts that night.
好。那我告诉你：11:30我接到匿名电话，立即出警。因为案情紧急，我一路飞车，最快速度赶到现场。中间没有耽误任何一秒。
Fine. I'll tell you: 11:30 I received an anonymous call and dispatched immediately. Because it was urgent, I drove as fast as possible. Didn't waste a single second.
最快速度？11:30出发，12:00到达。整整30分钟。但我查过地图，也问过Tommy，从你家到蓝月亮酒吧只需要15分钟。
Fastest speed? Departed 11:30, arrived 12:00. A full 30 minutes. But I checked the map and asked Tommy—from your house to Blue Moon takes only 15 minutes.
也许路上堵车！也许我记错了！这即使是法庭上也是允许的误差！
Maybe there was traffic! Maybe I misremembered! Such errors are admissible even in court!
深夜11点半的艾斯弗德会有这么严重的堵车？而且作为一名资深警探，你会把出警时间记错整整一倍？这可不是"误差"。
Serious traffic in Ashford at 11:30 PM? And as a veteran detective, you'd misremember your response time by double? That's no 'error'.
你到底想说什么？
What are you trying to say?
我想问那多出来的15分钟，你在哪里？在为了"最快速度"而特意绕路吗？或者...你在等我？等我先到，好实施你的计划？
I want to ask where you were for those extra 15 minutes. Taking a detour for 'fastest speed'? Or... were you waiting for me? Waiting for me to arrive first to execute your plan?
准备？！我准备了什么？！我他妈什么都没准备！就是接到电话穿上衣服就出门了！你以为我会提前知道今晚有人要死？！
Preparation?! What did I prepare?! I prepared absolutely nothing! Got the call, threw on my clothes and left! You think I knew someone was going to die tonight?!
没有准备？那你怎么解释这份记录？便携式现场勘验箱。你亲笔签名的领用单。
No preparation? Then how do you explain this requisition record? Portable Field Investigation Kit. Signed by you.
那...那又能说明什么？警局每天都有物资领用！我作为一名警探，领一套设备补充库存有什么问题？
So... so what does that prove? Supplies are requisitioned every day! As a detective, what's wrong with restocking a kit?
补充库存？确实没问题。但问题在于时间。看看日期：11月2日，19:30。
Restocking? Sure, no problem. But the problem is the time. Look at the date: November 2nd, 19:30.
案发前一天的晚上，而且是你休假的时候。你会特意跑回警局，去领一套并不是标配的高级勘验箱来"补充库存"？
The night before the crime, and on your day off. You'd specifically run back to the station to 'restock' a non-standard, high-end kit?
我...我只是刚好路过！我做事喜欢未雨绸缪！不行吗？
I... I was just passing by! I like to be prepared! Is that a crime?
不，这不叫未雨绸缪。这叫"按图索骥"。你提前领了它，是因为你知道第二天晚上你会需要它。Morrison，这不是突发情况，这是预谋。
No, that's not preparedness. That's 'following the plan'. You took it early because you knew you'd need it the next night. Morrison, this wasn't an emergency, it was premeditated.
那是...那是巧合！我只是恰好把它扔在车上了。但我到了现场根本没用它！当时情况太乱，我只是做了目视检查！
That's... that's a coincidence! I just happened to toss it in the car. But I didn't use it at the scene! It was too chaotic, I only did a visual inspection!
没有使用？那么请解释一下这张照片。Webb办公室地板上有几个清晰的压痕。
Didn't use it? Then please explain this photo. Clear indentations on the floor of Webb's office.
压痕？那可能是椅子，或者是桌腿！这就是你想说的证据？几条划痕？
Indentations? Could be a chair, or table legs! This is your evidence? A few scratches?
仔细看。四方形分布，四角有特殊的防滑垫印迹。这不是家具留下的。
Look closely. Square distribution, special anti-slip pad marks at corners. Not furniture.
这些印迹完美契合那个勘验箱的底部。位置就在办公桌旁，你最方便"布置"现场的地方。
These marks perfectly match that kit's bottom. Right next to the desk, the most convenient spot for you to 'stage' the scene.
那可能是我进去的时候随手放了一下！我累了，把箱子放地上休息一下，有问题吗？
Maybe I set it down when I went in! I was tired, rested the box on the floor, any problem?
对！那肯定是我检查现场时留下的！我把箱子放在地上取证了！这很正常！我是警察，我在履行职责！
Yes! That must be from when I checked the scene! I put the box down to collect evidence! That's normal! I'm a cop, doing my duty!
履行职责？酒吧的员工们可不这么说。
Doing your duty? The bar staff would disagree.
他们？哪怕是这群臭虫也不可能时刻盯着我！我是后来才拿进去的！
Them? Even those vermin couldn't watch me every second! I brought it in later!
不，他们看得很清楚。你冲进房间时，两手空空，除了手电筒什么都没拿。你根本没带箱子进去。
No, they saw clearly. You rushed into the room empty-handed, except for a flashlight. You never brought the box in.
别编了。没人看到那个箱子。所以这些压痕只有一个解释：它们是你"提前"留下的。在所有人发现尸体之前，你就在那里布置现场了。
Stop lying. No one saw that box. So these marks have only one explanation: you left them 'in advance'. You were staging the scene before the body was even discovered.
多年的警探生活让你形成了肌肉记忆，哪怕是栽赃嫁祸，你都忘不了按部就班地使用工具。这个压痕，就是你留下的签名。
Years of detective work gave you muscle memory. Even when framing someone, you couldn't forget to use your tools methodically. This indentation is your signature.
够了...别说了...
Enough... stop...
这些证据足够把你送上法庭。现在，告诉我，是谁雇你的？对方给了你多少钱？
This evidence is enough to send you to court. Now, tell me, who hired you? How much did they pay?
钱？你以为我是为了钱？Brennan，你根本不懂...
Money? You think I did it for money? Brennan, you don't understand...
不然呢？
What else?
我欠疤面Tony五千美元。他要杀我全家。我老婆，Brennan。我他妈的老婆。
I owe Scarface Tony five thousand dollars. He's going to kill my family. My wife, Brennan. My fucking wife.
所以你选择栽赃我。
So you chose to frame me.
我已经走投无路了。有个神秘人找到我，直接给了我五千美金现金。他说这笔钱可以帮我还债，保护我家人，我只需要把你变成嫌疑人。但这不是我的错！
I was desperate. A mysterious man found me, gave me 5k cash right there. Said it would cover my debt, protect my family. All I had to do was make you a suspect. But it's not my fault!
多么慷慨的提议。
What a generous offer.
你不懂！你他妈根本不懂！如果我不这么做，他们会杀了我全家！
You don't understand! You fucking don't understand! If I didn't do it, they'd kill my whole family!
我懂。我只是不同意。
I understand. I just don't agree.
不同意？那你告诉我，Brennan，如果是你的家人，你会怎么做？
Don't agree? Then tell me, Brennan, if it were your family, what would you do?
我不知道。但我绝不会为了保全自己，就把枪口对准并肩作战的同伴。Morrison，你有选择。你只是因为恐惧，就跪下了。
I don't know. But I would never turn my gun on a partner just to save myself. Morrison, you had a choice. You just knelt down because of fear.
恐惧？哈...等你到了我这个岁数，有了软肋，你也会跪下的。那个人知道我的一切，如果不照做，明天艾斯弗德河里就会多几具尸体。我没有别的路可走！

Fear? Hah... when you get to my age, when you have weaknesses, you'll kneel too. That man knew everything about me. If I didn't do it, there'd be more bodies in the Ashford River by tomorrow. I had no other path!
这就是你所有的自我辩护？
Is that your entire defense?
这叫规则。要么被吃，要么喂别人吃。良心是有钱人的奢侈品。
It's the rules. Eat or be eaten. Conscience is a luxury for the rich.
也许。但我还是要试试。告诉我那个人是谁？
Maybe. But I'll still try. Tell me, who is he?
我...我为什么要告诉你？
Why... why should I tell you?
因为如果你不告诉我，我会把这些证据交给你的上司。到时候，你不仅会失去工作，还会面临法律制裁。但如果你配合，我可以尽量帮你。
Because if you don't, I'll give this evidence to your superior. You'll lose your job and face charges. If you help, I'll try to help you.
我...我可以告诉你...但你必须保证...保证不会牵连到我。
I... I can tell you... but you must promise... promise it won't implicate me.
我不能保证。但我可以保证，如果你配合，我会尽量帮你。
I can't promise that. But if you cooperate, I'll do what I can.
我只知道他叫Whale。他从不露面。只有纸条，特殊纹样的纸条。
I only know he's called Whale. Never shows his face. Only notes, with a special pattern.
除了你，他还收买了谁？
Besides you, who else?
我不知道...他说会有人配合...Webb是别人杀的，我只负责栽赃你...
I don't know... he said someone would cooperate... Webb was killed by someone else, I was just the setup man...
双重保险。
Double insurance.
对。他从一开始就没信任过我。Brennan...他还说了一件事。
Right. He never trusted me. Brennan... he said one more thing.
什么？
What?
Webb约你去酒吧，不是要给你案子。他把你当成'保险侦探'。如果他出事，你一定会查到底。他利用了你的职业操守。
Webb didn't invite you to give you a case. You were his 'insurance detective'. If he died, you'd investigate. He used your professional integrity.
所以我也是工具。Webb的工具，Whale的目标。
So I'm a tool too. Webb's tool, Whale's target.
我们都是工具。Webb，我，你......每个人都是棋子。这个世界就是这样，Brennan。强者支配弱者，这是自然规律。我告诉你这些，不是因为别的...而是因为这对大家都好。继续对抗对谁都没好处。
We're all tools. Webb, me, you... everyone is a pawn. The world is like this, Brennan. The strong rule the weak, strict laws of nature. Give up, it's better for everyone.
不一样，Morrison。我被利用，但我没有出卖别人。你会为此付出代价。
Different, Morrison. I was used, but I didn't betray anyone. You'll pay for that.
代价？也许吧。但至少我现在还能保住我的位置。Brennan，我只是个执行者，真正的大人物在后面...你明白吗？
Pay? Maybe. But I keep my spot for now. Brennan, I'm just an executor. The big shots are behind... get it?
我当然明白。强者、弱者、执行者、工具......我在法庭、战壕和酒吧见过太多标签。但我更相信另外一种东西：有人总得站在真相那边。今天那个人是我。
I understand. Labels. But I believe someone has to stand for the truth. Today, that's me.
还有一件事。Webb的办公室，我需要进去看看。
One more thing. Webb's office—I need to take a look inside.
那个房间我也进不去。钥匙不在我这里。
I can't get into that room either. I don't have the key.
你是负责这个案子的警探。叫Tommy来开门。
You're the detective on this case. Get Tommy to open it.
......我可以打个电话。
...I can make a call.
那就打吧。我在门口等你。
Then make it. I'll wait at the door.
顺便说一句，Morrison——下次想撒谎，记得把细节也练熟点。魅力靠的是自信，谎言靠的是细节。你两样都不够。
By the way, Morrison—next time you lie, practice the details. Charm needs confidence, lies need details. You lack both.
Tommy，我们需要再谈谈Webb的生意。
Tommy, we need to talk about Webb's business again.
Brennan先生...我该说的都说了...都是私酒生意...
Mr. Brennan... I've said everything... it's all bootlegging business...
是吗？那解释一下这个。你说收入全部来自酒类销售。但这份报告显示，Webb每月收入15,000美元。
Is that so? Then explain this. You said all income comes from alcohol sales. But this report shows Webb earns $15,000 monthly.
那...那份报告一定是弄错了...我...我没有紧张...Webb先生只做私酒生意，绝对没有其他业务！
That... that report must be wrong... I... I'm not nervous... Mr. Webb only does bootlegging, absolutely no other business!
Tommy，你们的酒卖得不错。每月4,000美元，在禁酒令时代算是小康水平。可有个小问题——Webb每月收入是15,000。11,000美元的差额，你打算怎么解释？
Tommy, your liquor sells well. $4,000 a month. But there's a small problem—Webb's monthly income is $15,000. How do you explain the $11,000 difference?
那个...可能...可能是我算错了...
That... maybe... maybe I calculated it wrong...
算错？Tommy，我当过律师，最不怕的就是算账。11,000美元的差额，你做账三年，会'记错'一万多美元？
Calculated wrong? Tommy, I was a lawyer. The difference is $11,000. You've been keeping books for three years, you'd 'miscount' over ten thousand?
好吧...好吧...Webb先生还做...古董买卖...中国花瓶，欧洲油画...那些有钱人喜欢的东西...
Okay... okay... Mr. Webb also deals in... antiques... Chinese vases, European oil paintings... things rich people like...
古董。有意思。为什么一开始不说？
Antiques. Interesting. Why didn't you say so at first?
因为...因为那些客户都是体面人...Thompson议员，Coleman法官...Webb先生说要保密...不该问的不问...这是规矩...
Because... those clients are respectable people... Senator Thompson, Judge Coleman... Mr. Webb said to keep it secret... don't ask what shouldn't be asked... that's the rule...
根据我的调查，中国花瓶卖给了Thompson议员，欧洲油画卖给了Coleman法官。对吗？
According to my investigation, the Chinese vase was sold to Senator Thompson, and the European painting to Judge Coleman. Correct?
对...对...都已经卖出去了...
Yes... yes... they're all sold...
Tommy，我昨晚去歌舞厅拍了些照片。中国花瓶和欧洲油画，它们还在歌舞厅里。你是怎么卖'空气'的？
Tommy, I took some photos at the dance hall last night. The Chinese vase and the European painting are still there. How did you sell 'air'?
也许...也许Webb先生又买回来了...我...我只是按Webb先生的指示记账！他说这些是'特殊的商业安排'...让我记录成古董销售...

Maybe... maybe Mr. Webb bought them back... I... I just keep books according to Mr. Webb's instructions! He said these were 'special business arrangements'... asked me to record them as antique sales...
'特殊的商业安排'——这话听起来像黑手党拿来的。Tommy，你到底在帮Webb做什么？
'Special business arrangements'—sounds like something from the mob. Tommy, what exactly were you helping Webb with?
我不知道！我真的不知道！我不参与具体业务，只是做账...Webb先生从来不告诉我细节...
I don't know! I really don't know! I'm not involved in the business, just bookkeeping... Mr. Webb never tells me details...
这个工作日志在Webb的办公室，上面的笔迹和你办公室的本子上的笔迹一模一样。'Thompson，访问30分钟，重点观看中国花瓶'。一个'只做账'的人，会记录客人看什么、看多久吗？

This work log was in Webb's office. The handwriting matches the ledger in your office. 'Thompson, visited 30 mins, focused on Chinese vase'. Would a person 'just doing books' record what guests look at and for how long?
是...是Webb先生让我记的...
It... it was Mr. Webb who asked me to record it...
同一件古董被'卖'给同一个人多次。Tommy，那是按月收取的保护费。你管理着整个勒索网络的账目。每个客户，每笔交易，都在你这个'只做账的'脑子里。

The same antique 'sold' to the same person multiple times. Tommy, that's a monthly protection fee. You manage the accounts for the entire blackmail ring. Every client, every transaction, is in your head.
我没有选择！Webb先生说如果我不配合，就让我失业！我有家要养...我不能失去这份工作！
I had no choice! Mr. Webb said if I didn't cooperate, he'd fire me! I have a family to support... I can't lose this job!
所以你帮他勒索那些人。
So you helped him blackmail those people.
我知道这不对...但我能怎么办？我只是个打工的...我没有Webb的手段...
I know it's wrong... but what could I do? I'm just an employee... I don't have Webb's means...
Tommy，你会为此付出代价。但如果你配合调查...至少不会像Webb那样。
Tommy, you will pay for this. But if you cooperate... at least you won't end up like Webb.
我...我会配合...我什么都说...那些不是真正的古董销售...每个古董就是一个档案的代号...中国花瓶代表Thompson议员，欧洲油画代表Coleman法官...
I... I will cooperate... I'll say everything... those weren't real antique sales... each antique was a code for a file... Chinese vase for Senator Thompson, European painting for Judge Coleman...
五年。你做这个，做了整整五年。
Five years. You've been doing this for five whole years.
我...我知道错了...我会付出代价的...
I... I know I was wrong... I will pay the price...
现在告诉我——Webb最近勒索的那个人，是谁？
Now tell me—who was Webb blackmailing recently?
我...我真的不知道他的名字...Webb先生从来不说...
I... I really don't know his name... Mr. Webb never said...
但你知道Webb遇到麻烦了。
But you knew Webb was in trouble.
对...他最近...很不对劲...那种紧张我以前从没见过...
Yes... he was... very off recently... a kind of nervousness I've never seen before...
多不对劲？
How off?
他...他开始锁门。以前Webb先生从不锁门，现在连去趟厕所都要把办公室锁上。还有...他开始备枪。Brennan先生，他真的很害怕。
He... he started locking the door. Mr. Webb never locked his door before, now he locks it even to go to the toilet. And... he started carrying a gun. Mr. Brennan, he was really scared.
Webb害怕。有意思。一个靠威胁别人活了五年的人，终于遇到更可怕的了。
Webb scared. Interesting. A man who lived by threatening others for five years finally met something scarier.
如果连Webb先生都害怕...那说明对方真的...真的很危险...
If even Mr. Webb was scared... that means the person is... really dangerous...
除了你，还有谁了解Webb的生意？
Besides you, who else knows about Webb's business?
Jimmy...厨师Jimmy...
Jimmy... Jimmy the cook...
厨师？账本上有他的记录吗？
The cook? Any records of him in the ledger?
Jimmy的工资高的离谱...Webb先生嘱咐我加的...应该是有什么问题...
Jimmy's salary is ridiculously high... Mr. Webb instructed me to add it... There must be something wrong...
高多少？
How much higher?
一个月500美金...其他厨师只有120...就算是主厨，也高得离谱...我问过Webb先生，他说不用管，照做就行...
$500 a month... other cooks only get $120... even for a head chef, it's ridiculously high... I asked Mr. Webb, he said don't worry, just do as told...
4倍工资。一个厨师拿这么多钱。你确定Jimmy只是厨师？
4 times the salary. A cook getting paid this much. Are you sure Jimmy is just a cook?
Brennan先生...我能说的都说了...剩下的，您得去问Jimmy...
Mr. Brennan... I've said all I can... for the rest, you have to ask Jimmy...
Jimmy，我们聊聊。
Jimmy, let's talk.
正在忙。厨房还有活。
Busy. Still work in kitchen.
Webb死了。你不想说点什么吗？
Webb is dead. Don't you want to say something?
Webb先生...我知道他死了。我很难过。但我不知道该说什么，我只是个厨子。
Mr. Webb... I know he's dead. I'm sad. But I don't know what to say, I'm just a cook.
一个优秀员工。我有奖状，还有推荐信...我自己写的，但是真的。我每天努力工作，忠诚，守规矩。我没做过任何坏事。
An excellent employee. I have awards, recommendation letters... wrote them myself, but true. I work hard every day, loyal, follow rules. Never did anything bad.
忠诚的员工？没做过坏事？那三天前发生的事怎么解释？
Loyal employee? Never did bad? Then explain what happened three days ago?
Tommy亲眼看见你从Webb办公室出来，怀里抱着东西，还要躲着他。优秀员工会偷老板的东西吗？
Tommy saw you coming out of Webb's office, carrying something, hiding from him. Do excellent employees steal from their boss?
那...那不是偷...拿了...以为...不要的...那是垃圾...堆在角落...有灰...
That, that wasn't stealing, took it, thought unwanted, garbage, in corner, dusty...
我不知道那是古董。我只是觉得...虽然破...但洗干净能给Anna插花。
I didn't know it was an antique, I just thought, broken as it is, cleaned up, Anna could use it for flowers.
你说你不知道它的价值？那你怎么解释这个？
You say you didn't know its value? Then explain this.
这张纸是在你家里发现的，从Webb的古董鉴定书上撕下来的——Bennington陶壶，价值两千美元。你天天在这工作，会不知道它的价值？
This paper was found at your home. Torn from Webb's antique appraisal book - Bennington Jug, worth $2000. You work here every day, and you didn't know its value?
Anna...快生了。家里...没钱。申请身份...被拒了...需要钱...
Anna... is due soon. No money... at home. Citizenship application... rejected... need money...
所以你为了钱背叛了Webb？
So you betrayed Webb for money?
不！我没有背叛！Webb对我很好...送书给我...教我英语...
No! I didn't betray him! Webb was good to me, gave me books, taught me English...
也就是偷了这个壶...我没想害他。我们关系很好。我发誓！我一直把他当恩人！
Just stole this jug, didn't mean to harm him, we were good, I swear! Always saw him as a benefactor!
恩人？那Vivian听到的是什么？
Benefactor? Then what did Vivian hear?
Vivian亲耳听到你在Webb办公室里大吼——'别接那单，会出大事的'。你不是在和你的恩人叙旧，你是在和他争吵。而且是非常激烈的争吵。
Vivian heard you screaming in Webb's office—'Don't take that deal, it'll cause big trouble'. You weren't catching up with your benefactor, you were arguing. A very heated argument.
您...怎么知道...
How... how do you know...
Webb死了。正如你所预料的那样。你那时候就知道他会死，因为你知道'Whale'是谁。
Webb is dead. Just as you predicted. You knew back then he would die, because you know who 'Whale' is.
别说那个名字...求您...别说了...
Don't say that name... Please... stop...
你在保护谁？Anna？
Who are you protecting? Anna?
我保护不了任何人！Webb以为有证据就安全了，结果他死了！Morrison就在门外，却什么都'没看见'！
I can't protect anyone! Webb thought evidence made him safe, but he died! Morrison was right outside and 'saw nothing'!
'Whale'的人到处都是...警察、法官、银行家...我只是个厨师...我能做什么？我只想带Anna走...
'Whale's' people are everywhere, police, judges, bankers, I'm just a cook, what can I do? I just want to take Anna away...
你知道'Whale'是谁吗？
Do you know who 'Whale' is?
只知道名字...Webb说这是'最大的一笔生意'...做成了就一辈子不愁...我求他别做...会死人...
Only know the name... Webb said it was 'the biggest deal'... set for life if done... I begged him not to... people will die...
求您了...别问了...如果是为了Anna，让我走吧...
Please... stop asking... if for Anna's sake, let me go...
回厨房去吧。把今天的活干完。
Go back to the kitchen. Finish your work for today.
谢谢...谢谢...我一定会干完的...
Thank you... thank you... I'll finish it...
但别试图逃跑。你逃不掉的。
But don't try to run. You can't escape.
我知道...我不会跑...
I know... I won't run...
Vivian，我得问你几个问题。
Vivian, I have a few questions.
律师要在旁边吗？我不过是唱歌的，Brennan先生。
Do I need a lawyer? I'm just a singer, Mr. Brennan.
通常唱歌的不会这副表情——看起来像是刚卸下了一个巨大的包袱。
Singers don't usually look like they just unloaded a huge burden.
你在胡说什么？我只是在排练，又回到舞台上了，感觉稍微自在一点了。
What are you talking about? I'm just rehearsing. Being back on stage feels a bit more comfortable now.
别跟我演戏。回答问题：你和Webb现在到底是什么关系？
Don't act with me. Answer the question: What is your relationship with Webb now?
曾经他出钱，我出声线，仅此而已。我依然深爱着他，他的死让我很伤心。
He paid, I sang. That's all. I still love him deeply, his death broke me.
"深爱"？这封信可不是这么说的。"已无法忍受"、"彻底结束"... 这些字眼看起来不像是情书，更像是分手信。
'Deep love'? This letter says otherwise. 'Unbearable', 'Completely over'... These words look more like a breakup letter than a love letter.
每个人情绪失控的时候都会写点蠢东西。那封信我从来没打算给他看，我就随手扔在废纸篓里了。
Everyone writes stupid things when emotional. I never planned to show him. Just tossed it in the bin.
只有废纸篓？我在你的梳妆台上发现的，折叠得很整齐。如果你真的只是发泄情绪，为什么不把它撕了或者烧了？这看起来像是在犹豫要不要寄出去。
Just the bin? Found it on your dresser, neatly folded. If just venting, why not tear or burn it? Looks like hesitation to send.
你不懂女人，侦探。有些东西留着是为了提醒自己......提醒自己忍耐是有极限的。但这不代表我不爱他了。
You don't understand women. Kept it as a reminder... that patience has limits. Doesn't mean I don't love him.
忍耐？你说Webb向你承诺过什么？
Patience? What did Webb promise you?
他承诺带我离开这一行。我们和好了，信只是过去式。我们甚至已经预订了下周去法国的船票，打算去那里重新开始。
Promised to take me away. We reconciled, letter is past. Even booked tickets to France for next week. Fresh start.
如果计划还在，为什么我在你的垃圾桶里找到了被撕碎的船票？
If the plan stands, why did I find torn ticket scraps in your trash?
撕票说明旅行取消，不说明我杀了他。
Torn means cancelled, doesn't mean I killed him.
撕掉去法国的船票，又对他充满怨恨...你对Webb的感情，比你说的要复杂得多。
Tore up the tickets to France, filled with resentment toward him... Your feelings for Webb are far more complicated than you're letting on.
感情？我只是一个被骗的傻女人。
Feelings? I'm just a foolish woman who got played.
被骗？被骗的人会亲手撕掉两个人的未来？你不是伤心，你是愤怒。
Played? Would someone who got played tear up their shared future with her own hands? You weren't sad. You were furious.
吵架而已！每对情侣都会吵架。我们只是...有些分歧需要解决。
Just a fight! Every couple fights. We just... had some differences to work out.
分歧？我看到的是仇恨。
Differences? What I see is hatred.
我没有恨他到那种程度。我对他只有失望，没有仇恨。我从没想过要报复他。
I didn't hate him that much. I was only disappointed in him, not hateful. I never thought about getting revenge.
你的日记里写得很清楚：'谁背叛我，我就让他痛不欲生'。
Your diary says it clearly: 'Whoever betrays me, I will make them suffer'.
日记。你在我最糟糕的一天里偷听我脑子里的脏话，这很英勇？
Diary. Eavesdropping on my dark thoughts on my worst day. Heroic?
Tommy告诉我，Webb最近和Rita打得火热。这就是'谁背叛我'的那个人——Webb。你恨他。
Tommy told me Webb's been hot with Rita lately. That's who 'betrayed you'—Webb. You hated him.
Webb换投资项目而已。他出钱买下我，我给他利润。这骗局从第一天就存在，我并不惊讶。
Webb just changing investments. He bought me, I gave profit. Scam since day one, not surprised.
Tommy说他看到你站在走廊，看着他们。
Tommy saw you in hallway, watching them.
是...我看到了。那天我在走廊看到他和Rita...他对她笑的样子...看起来那么温柔。我站在那里，像个多余的人。
Yes... I saw. Him and Rita... his smile... so gentle. I stood there, like a spare part.
但这又能说明什么？他不欠我什么。我们只是交易关系。我早就知道了，不是吗？
But what does it prove? He owes me nothing. Just business. I knew it, didn't I?
那你为什么说'我为他做了那么多'？
Then why did you say 'I did so much for him'?
因为我把一生抵押给他，换来的只是一张过期船票。这叫投入产出比，Brennan先生。不是什么廉价的感情故事。
Because I mortgaged my life to him, for an expired ticket. That's ROI, Mr. Brennan. Not a cheap romance.
Rosa说，大概11点左右，她在清理走廊的时候，看见你神情紧张地快步走向会客室。
Rosa said around 11 o'clock, while cleaning the hallway, she saw you walking tensely towards the parlor.
我确实去了门口。敲门，他在里面吼'别打扰我'。门锁着，我没见到他，就...离开了。
I went. Knocked, he shouted 'Don't disturb me'. Locked. Didn't see him, so... I left.
你恨他。
You hate him.
恨过。也想过——如果他死了，一切会不会干净一些。但那只是在脑子里走了一遍。每个被背叛的女人都这么想过。
Hated him. Thought about him dying. But only in my head. Every betrayed woman thinks that.
最后一次接触就是那句'别打扰我'？
Last contact was 'Don't disturb me'?
是。11点05分，会客室门口。我被他像赶走一只流浪狗一样赶走。然后我转身，离开。之后发生什么，不关我的事。
Yes. 11:05. Chased away like a stray dog. Turned and left. Can't care about what happened next.
你坚持没杀Webb。
You insist you didn't kill him.
我承认我恨他，承认我幻想过他死，承认我为这个幻想设计过完美的计划。但我没有动手。你想要的供词——一个杀人犯的供词——我没有。
Admit hate, fantasy, plan. But no action. The confession you want - murderer's confession - I don't have.
我只是一个...被背叛的傻女人。
I'm just a... betrayed stupid woman.
等一下，Vivian。我在Webb办公室发现了一张便条。上面写着：'保险箱密码是我们开始的那一天。那一刻是真的。'我猜...这是写给你的。
Wait, Vivian. I found a note in Webb's office. It says: 'Safe code is day we started. That moment was real.' I think... this was meant for you.
这...这是什么意思...？'那一刻是真的'...？
What... what does this mean...? 'That moment was real'...?
Tommy告诉我，Webb知道Rita的真实身份。他故意和Rita走近，是为了保护你。Webb和Rita的关系是假的。
Tommy says Webb knew Rita's identity. Got close to protect you. Relationship was fake.
我以为...我以为他背叛了我...我看到的那些...原来都是他演给'Whale'看的...
I thought... I thought he betrayed me... What I saw... all an act for 'Whale'...
还有这个项链。'给我最爱的Vivian，永远的舞台。'
And this necklace. 'To my dearest Vivian, forever stage.'
那是谎言。Webb从来没有兑现过任何承诺。
That's a lie. Webb never kept promises.
也许不全是谎言。便条上写：'密码是我们开始的那一天。那一刻是真的。'
Maybe not all lies. Note says: 'Code is start day. Moment was real.'
那是真的...
That was real...
那个时刻是什么时候？
When was that moment?
1920. August 7th.
发生了什么？
What happened?
那年我17岁，父母把我卖给妓院。在路上...Webb出现了，把我带到了蓝月亮。8月7日那天，我第一次在舞台上唱歌。
17. Parents sold me to brothel. Webb appeared on the way, took me to Blue Moon. Aug 7th, first time singing on stage.
那一刻...我以为自己终于不是商品了。我以为Webb和其他男人不一样。
That moment... thought I wasn't a commodity anymore. Thought Webb was different.
但酒吧开了不到半年，他就开始勒索客人。而我...我负责陪那些VIP，套取秘密。我以为的'舞台'，不过是另一种牢笼。
But half year later, he blackmailed guests. I... I accompanied VIPs, stole secrets. 'Stage' was just another cage.
但他还记得那个时刻。否则不会用那个日期做密码。
But he remembered. Or he wouldn't use that date as code.
可那又怎么样？他把我从一个牢笼带到另一个牢笼。
So what? He took me from one cage to another.
你说得对。但至少...那个时刻可能是真的。
You're right. But at least... that moment might have been real.
0807。1920年8月7日。那是他人生中唯一值得记住的时刻。现在你可以走了。
0807. Aug 7th 1920. Only moment worth remembering. Now you can leave.
Jimmy。
Jimmy.
我知道！没杀人。11点半...枪响。大家都听到了。我在厨房，有人证。
I know! No kill. 11:30... gunshot. Everyone hear. I in kitchen. Witness.
11点半。你怎么这么确定时间？
11:30. How are you so sure about the time?
大家...大家都听到了。问谁都一样。
Everyone... everyone heard. Ask anyone.
枪声从哪个方向传来的？
Which direction did the gunshot come from?
Webb的...Webb会客室。楼下。
Webb's... Webb's parlor. Downstairs.
你确定？
You sure?
我...确定。为什么这样问？
I... sure. Why ask like that?
因为我去看了Webb办公室的窗户。
Because I checked the window in Webb's office.
Window...?
看这个弹孔——是从上向下射击的。窗户没有破损，但根据弹孔的痕迹来看，也是比较新的，最近才留下的。
Look at this bullet hole—shot from top to bottom. The window has no damage, but judging from the bullet hole, it's also quite fresh, left recently.
这...这不能说明什么。也许...也许有人在外面开枪？
This... proves nothing. Maybe... someone shot from outside?
是的，Jimmy。11点半的枪声，是伪造的。
Yes, Jimmy. The 11:30 gunshot was faked.
不可能！如果有枪声...能听到。酒吧很小。藏不住。
Impossible! If gunshot... we hear. Bar small. Cannot hide.
是吗？当晚11点的时候，你在哪？
Really? Where were you at 11 that night?
厨房...我一直在厨房。
Kitchen... I was in kitchen.
11点到11点10分，舞台在演什么节目？
11:00 to 11:10, what was playing on stage?
我...不记得。很多节目。
I... don't remember. Many shows.
《艾斯弗德狂想》。Rosa说那晚的鼓点震耳欲聋。
'Ashford Rhapsody'. Rosa said the drums were deafening that night.
所以...所以什么？
So... so what?
这个开场曲是Vivian安排的。但你利用了它。在最强鼓点的时候，就算开枪也不会被听到。
Vivian arranged this opener. But you exploited it. During the loudest drums, even a gunshot wouldn't be heard.
你...你在说什么...我听不懂...
You... what are you saying... I don't understand...
你听得懂，Jimmy。11点，真正的枪声。11点半，假的枪声。
You understand, Jimmy. 11:00, the real shot. 11:30, the fake one.
就算音乐...但Vivian能证明！11点...她敲门了。听到了Webb的声音！
Even if music... but Vivian prove! 11:00... she knock. Hear Webb voice!
Vivian。对，她确实敲门了。11点05分。
Vivian. Yes, she did knock. 11:05.
对！她听到Webb说话了！Webb那时候还活着！
Yes! She heard Webb talk! Webb was alive then!
是隔着门听到的。而且当时音乐声音那么大，也不能完全听清是谁。
It was heard through the door. And the music was so loud at the time, couldn't clearly tell who it was.
就是Webb先生的声音！
It was Mr. Webb's voice!
Jimmy，你怎么会知道？你当时就在里面，不是吗？
Jimmy, how would you know? You were inside at the time, weren't you?
不...11点的时候我在和Tom一起送餐！他可以作证！
No... At 11, I was delivering food with Tom! He can testify!
送餐记录显示当时送餐只有你自己一个人。你就是这段时间来到Webb的办公室杀害了他。
The delivery log shows you were alone. That's when you went to Webb's office and killed him.
11点，你杀了他。11点05分，Vivian敲门，你锁着门，模仿他的声音让她离开。11点30分，你制造假枪声。
11:00, you killed him. 11:05, Vivian knocked, you locked door, mimicked him to make her leave. 11:30, you faked the shot.
不...为什么？没有杀他的理由！他对我有恩！
No... Why? No reason to kill him! He good to me!
有恩？那这是什么？
Good to you? Then what's this?
This... this...
11月1日。Whale的那笔5000美金。Webb没收到。
November 1st. That $5000 from Whale. Webb didn't get it.
那是...那是别的钱...
That was... other money...
那是给你的杀人定金。你为了钱背叛了Webb，这也成了你给Whale的投名状。
That was your deposit for murder. You betrayed Webb for money. That became your ticket to Whale.
不...不是这样的...
No... not like that...
而且...你还帮Whale清理了Webb的保险箱。Webb把关键证据——录音、文件——都锁在里面。案发后都被烧毁了。
And... you cleaned Webb's safe for Whale. Webb locked evidence inside - recordings, files. All burnt after the murder.
只有你有机会拿到密码。Jimmy。
Only you could get the code. Jimmy.
……是的……是的，都是……真的……
Hah... yes... yes, it's... all true...
跟我回警局。指证Whale。你和Anna可以进证人保护。
Come to the station with me. Testify against Whale. You and Anna can get witness protection.
证人保护？Morrison都做了伪证……警局里都是Whale的人……你护得了我们吗？
Witness protection? Morrison already committed perjury... the station is full of Whale's people... can you really protect us?
我活着……Anna就永远是杀人犯的妻子……孩子就永远有个杀人犯父亲……
If I live... Anna will forever be a killer's wife... the children will forever have a killer for a father...
你还有机会——
You still have a chance—
没有了。
No more.
保护？警局...全是Whale的人。Morrison就是。
Protection? Police... full of Whale's men. Morrison is one.
我可以——
I can—
你保护不了我们。如果我被抓...Whale会对Anna下手。我的孩子...
You can't protect us. If I'm caught... Whale will hurt Anna. My child...
Jimmy...你在想什么？
Jimmy... what are you thinking?
我的孩子需要的是一个好父亲...不是一个杀人犯。
My child needs... a good father... not a murderer.
Jimmy，别——
Jimmy, don't—
这是我唯一能保护他们的方式了。
This is the only way I can protect them now.
你还好吗？
Are you okay?
谢了。
Thanks.
你到底拍到什么了？
What exactly did you photograph?
一个模糊的背影。不够当证据，但够吓唬Morrison。
A blurry silhouette. Not enough for evidence, but enough to scare Morrison.
所以你在赌。
So you were bluffing.
我们都在赌。
We're both gambling.
你要去哪儿？
Where are you going?
找出谁陷害我。
To find out who framed me.
一个人？72小时？
Alone? In 72 hours?
我习惯一个人。
I'm used to being alone.
Morrison明显有问题。他来得太快，太急于定你的罪。
Morrison is clearly suspicious. He arrived too fast, too eager to pin this on you.
所以？
So?
所以这不只是一起简单的谋杀。背后有更大的事。
So this isn't just a simple murder. There's something bigger behind it.
那是你的新闻。不是我的问题。
That's your story. Not my problem.
我也在调查Webb。《艾斯弗德先驱报》记者Emma O'Malley——负责社会版。
I've been investigating Webb too. Emma O'Malley, Ashford Herald reporter. Society section.
但这不代表我只会写婚礼报道。
But that doesn't mean I only write wedding announcements.
社会版记者？Webb的案子可不是什么社交八卦。
Society reporter? Webb's case isn't some social gossip.
我知道。但Zack Brennan，Kirkland的前助理律师，因为'太有原则'被律所踢出来。
I know. But Zack Brennan, former associate at Kirkland, kicked out for being 'too principled.'
现在开着破旧侦探事务所接些离婚调查的私家侦探——您觉得自己现在有资格挑剔帮手吗？
Now running a shabby detective agency taking divorce cases. Do you really have the luxury to be picky about help?
...你做过功课。
...You've done your homework.
当然。您曾经很出色。圣母大学法学院全优毕业，在Kirkland工作时处理过不少棘手案件。
Of course. You were brilliant once. Graduated top of your class at Notre Dame Law. Handled tough cases at Kirkland.
只是您不懂得什么时候该闭嘴。
You just never knew when to keep your mouth shut.
'曾经'。这个词很重要，O'Malley小姐。
'Once.' That word matters, Miss O'Malley.
但您的专业能力还在。我需要这个。而您——需要不进监狱。
But your skills are still there. I need that. And you—need to stay out of prison.
听着，我知道您不信任我。我也不指望您信任我。
Look, I know you don't trust me. I don't expect you to.
但现在您没有选择。Morrison盯上您了，72小时根本不够。
But right now you have no choice. Morrison's got his eye on you, and 72 hours isn't nearly enough.
...你想要什么？
...What do you want?
真相。还有一个好故事。
The truth. And a good story.
至少你诚实。
At least you're honest.
这是答应了？
Is that a yes?
我还有选择吗？
Do I have a choice?
那我们从哪儿开始？
Then where do we start?
有人让我进去，有人打晕我，有人布置现场。
Someone let me in. Someone knocked me out. Someone staged the scene.
Webb的员工都看到我来了。酒保、门童、歌女、厨师...他们都知道些什么。
Webb's staff all saw me arrive. Bartender, doorman, singer, cook... They all know something.
好。我来问，你观察。
Good. I'll ask the questions, you observe.
我说了算。
I call the shots.
当然。你是专家。
Of course. You're the expert.
我一定是疯了...
I must be crazy...
我们都是。
We both are.
Mr. Brennan，您看这个工作记录卡。Rosa说她整晚在地下室走廊，但记录显示她在一楼走廊。
Mr. Brennan, look at this work record. Rosa said she was in the basement hallway all night, but the record shows she was in the first-floor hallway.
时间地点对不上。
The time and place don't match up.
还有这个毛巾，上面有着奇怪的味道，走廊的垃圾桶里还有氯仿瓶，那是能够迷晕人用的东西。
And this towel... it has a strange smell. There was a chloroform bottle in the hallway trash can too... that's something used to knock people out.
Rosa在撒谎。她知道的比她说的多。
Rosa is lying. She knows more than she's saying.
但是...她女儿的照片，还有那些医院账单...她看起来很绝望。
But... the photo of her daughter, and those hospital bills... She looks desperate.
绝望的人做绝望的事。不代表她没有撒谎。
Desperate people do desperate things. Doesn't mean she isn't lying.
我知道。只是...也许她是被逼的？
I know. It's just... maybe she was forced?
那就去问清楚。她在隐瞒什么，为什么隐瞒。
Then go ask and find out. What she's hiding, and why.
您打算怎么问？直接对质？
How do you intend to ask? Direct confrontation?
一步步来。先击破她的地点谎言，然后看她怎么圆。
Step by step. First break her location lie, then see how she spins it.
如果她崩溃了怎么办？
What if she breaks down?
如果她真是被迫的，崩溃反而是解脱。走吧。
If she really was forced, breaking down would be a relief. Let's go.
Mr. Brennan，您处理过类似的案子吗？被威胁配合犯罪的证人？
Mr. Brennan, have you handled cases like this? Witnesses threatened into cooperating with a crime?
见得多了。这城市不缺被逼无奈的人。
Seen plenty. This city has no shortage of desperate people.
Morrison...警察...一个警探...
Morrison... police... a detective...
很有可能。这里是艾斯弗德。
Very likely. This is Ashford.
但他应该调查案件，不是制造案件！他戴着警徽，宣过誓——
But he should be investigating crimes, not creating them! He wears a badge, he swore an oath—
Miss O'Malley，您觉得那身制服意味着什么？正义？在这个城市，它只是更好用的伪装罢了。
Miss O'Malley, what do you think that uniform means? Justice? In this city, it's just a better camouflage.
所以Morrison栽赃您，威胁Rosa...都是为了保护真凶？
So Morrison framing you, threatening Rosa... it's all to protect the real culprit?
一个警探冒这么大风险，要么是钱，要么是命。
A detective taking such a big risk, it's either for money or for his life.
您觉得Morrison背后还有人？
You think there's someone behind Morrison?
一个警探没有理由杀Webb。但如果有人出价够高，或者威胁够狠...那就是另一回事了。
A detective has no reason to kill Webb. But if someone pays enough, or threatens hard enough... that's a different story.
那我们必须找到那个人。
Then we must find that person.
先找Morrison的证据。财务记录、通话记录、他接触过的人...一定有破绽。
First find evidence on Morrison. Financial records, call logs, people he's contacted... there must be a flaw.
Mr. Brennan，等等。如果Morrison真的是凶手，他现在还戴着警徽。他可以销毁证据，威胁证人，甚至...威胁我们。
Mr. Brennan, wait. If Morrison really is the killer, he's still wearing that badge. He can destroy evidence, threaten witnesses, even... threaten us.
你害怕了吗
Are you scared?
不。只是...我从没对抗过警察。
No. Just... I've never gone up against a cop before.
那就当作对抗一个穿警服的罪犯。Miss O'Malley，您不是想要个好故事吗？这就是了。
Then treat it like going up against a criminal in a police uniform. Miss O'Malley, didn't you want a good story? This is it.
您说得对。从哪里开始？
You're right. Where do we start?
Morrison一定在家里藏了证据——收贿的钱、交易记录、或者联系人的信息。
Morrison surely hid evidence at home—bribe money, transaction records, or contact info.
您是说...去他家？但怎么进去？我们总不能偷偷潜入吧？
You mean... go to his house? But how do we get in? We can't just sneak in, can we?
Miss O'Malley，您是记者。用您的职业身份。
Miss O'Malley, you're a reporter. Use your professional identity.
采访...您是说，我可以假装采访警探家属？'英勇警探破获大案，专访警探妻子的不易'。
Interview... You mean I can pretend to interview a detective's family? 'Heroic Detective Cracks Case, Exclusive on Wife's Hardships'.
您的报社应该喜欢这种温情报道。您负责和Morrison太太聊天，我负责搜证。
Your paper should love that kind of heartwarming story. You chat with Mrs. Morrison, I handle the search.
您？Morrison太太会认出您吧？'嫌疑人'上门采访？
You? Mrs. Morrison will recognize you, right? 'Suspect' comes for an interview?
她不一定见过我。而且我可以是您的摄影助理。
She hasn't necessarily seen me. Besides, I can be your photography assistant.
如果Morrison突然回家怎么办？
What if Morrison suddenly comes home?
那我们就是两个无辜的记者，正在做温情报道。Morrison家在北区，他应该还在警局'处理案件'。
Then we're just two innocent reporters doing a heartwarming piece. Morrison lies in the North Side; he should still be at the station 'handling cases'.
好吧。那我们现在就去Morrison家？
Alright. So we go to Morrison's house now?
...Let's go.
Mr. Brennan，您刚才...对Rosa很温柔。
Mr. Brennan, you were... very gentle with Rosa just now.
她是受害者。
She is a victim.
所以您相信她？
So you believe her?
我相信证据。证据说她拖不动我。至于温柔...那是您的误会，Miss O'Malley。
I believe evidence. Evidence says she can't drag me. As for gentle... that's your misunderstanding, Miss O'Malley.
不，我看得很清楚。您说'你是个好母亲'的时候——
No, I saw it clearly. When you said 'You're a good mother'—
我只是陈述事实。别把我想得太复杂。
I was just stating a fact. Don't overcomplicate me.
好吧。如您所说。走吧。
Fine. As you say. Let's go.
在艾斯弗德，良心是奢侈品，而比起良心，大家更喜欢绿色的钞票。
In Ashford, conscience is a luxury. And people prefer green bills over conscience.
我不止一次见过这种情况。一旦沾上了那种灰色的东西，就再也没法洗干净了。
I've seen this more than once. Once you touch that grey stuff, you can never wash it off.
这是个天才的计划。您发挥特长，我发挥...特长。完美互补。
It's a genius plan. You use your specialty, I use... mine. Perfect complement.
别担心。如果被发现了，我就说我是您的狂热粉丝，跟踪狂的那种。
Don't worry. If we get caught, I'll say I'm your fanatical fan, the stalker kind.
私家侦探，Zack Brennan。……你是谁？这么晚了，在这里做什么？
Private detective, Zack Brennan... Who are you? And what are you doing here so late?
对...对不起先生！我...我是Rosa...只是个做清洁的...
S-sorry sir! I... I'm Rosa... just a cleaner...
现在几点？
What time is it now?
没...没有！什么都没看到！我一直在地下室走廊打扫...从十点开始...不，是九点半...Webb先生要求月底彻底清洁...
No... nothing! Didn't see anything! I was in the basement corridor the whole time cleaning... started at ten... no, nine-thirty... Mr. Webb requires thorough cleaning at month end...
放轻松，我就随便问问。
Relax, just asking.
有人能证明吗？
Anyone who can vouch for that?
没...没有人...地下室很少有人去...
No... no one... the basement rarely has visitors...
刚才你看到什么了吗？
Did you see anything earlier?
什么都没看到！我什么都...没看到！地下室很安静...就我一个人...一直在擦地板...什么都没看到...
Didn't see anything! I didn't... didn't see anything! The basement was quiet... just me alone... scrubbing the floor... didn't see anything...
我还没问你看到什么。
I haven't asked you what you saw yet.
我是说...地下室很深...隔音好...就算楼上有什么动静...我也什么都不知道...真的！
I mean... the basement is deep... good soundproofing... even if there was noise upstairs... I wouldn't know anything... really!
先生，求您了...我有个孩子要养...我不能出事...
Please, sir... I have a child to raise... I can't get into trouble...
我可没那么多耐心。撒谎只会让事情更复杂。
I don't have much patience. Lying will only make things more complicated.
我...我真的不能说...他们会杀了我的...我有孩子...求您了...我什么都没看到...
I... I really can't say... they'll kill me... I have a child... please... I didn't see anything...
应该一点多了...
Should be past one...
酒吧营业到这么晚？
The bar stays open this late?
不会...现在就我们几个员工还在了应该...
No... just a few of us staff still here now, I think...
有看到什么吗？
Did you see anything?
没有人能证明，所以你说什么我都得信？
No one can prove it, so I just have to take your word for it?
不不不...先生，地下室真的很少有人去的...那里又暗又潮...除了我没人愿意下去打扫...您可以问问别人...Webb先生每个月都让我去...三年了...我从来没骗过人...

No no no... sir, the basement really rarely has visitors... it's dark and damp down there... no one else wants to go clean... you can ask others... Mr. Webb has me go every month... three years... I've never lied to anyone...
没人能证明，就是没人能证明。
No one can prove it means no one can prove it.
先生...我...我发誓...求您了...我有个孩子要养...她才8岁...我不能出事...我真的什么都没看到...我只是在打扫...
Sir... I... I swear... please... I have a child to raise... she's only eight... I can't get into trouble... I really didn't see anything... I was just cleaning...
...Fine.
不习惯被人问话？还是有什么不能说的？
Not used to being questioned? Or is there something you can't say?
我没有！我什么都不知道！先生...您不知道这里是什么地方...我只是个打扫的...我什么都没看到...求您了...
I don't! I don't know anything! Sir... you don't know what kind of place this is... I'm just a cleaner... I didn't see anything... please...
这里是什么地方？
What kind of place is this?
我...我不能说...他们会杀了我的...我有孩子...她才8岁...我不能出事...先生...他们什么都做得出来...
I... I can't say... they'll kill me... I have a child... she's only eight... I can't get into trouble... sir... they're capable of anything...
谁会杀了你？
Who would kill you?
我不能说...我不能说...求您放过我...我什么都没看到...什么都不知道...求您了...
I can't say... I can't say... please let me go... I didn't see anything... don't know anything... please...
行了。别让人以为我欺负你。但我会盯着你的。
Enough. Don't make it look like I'm bullying you. But I'll be watching.
你刚才说漏嘴了。
You just let something slip.
我没有！先生，我真的什么都不知道！我就是在打扫...三年了...我能知道什么呢...
I didn't! Sir, I really don't know anything! I was just cleaning... three years... what could I possibly know...
没事，你慢慢说。
It's okay, take your time.
我...我真的什么都不知道...三年了...我只管擦地板...
I... I really don't know anything... three years... I just mop the floors...
行吧。
Fine.
谢...谢谢您...我...您可以去会客室附近看看...
Th-thank you... I... You could check around the reception room...
记住，有些事情不知道比较安全。
Remember, some things are safer not knowing.
我什么都不知道...
I don't know anything...
会客室？
The reception room?
我什么都没说...您什么都没听到...
I didn't say anything... You didn't hear anything...
您...您又来了？我之前都已经告诉您了...
You... you're back? I already told you everything...
我还有几个问题想确认一下。
I have a few more questions to confirm.
好...好的，您请问吧...
O-okay... please go ahead...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 有人能证明你在哪里吗？

Words: 什么...该死...
What... damn...
Webb？！不...这不对...
Webb?! No... this isn't right...
我敲门...然后...
I knocked... then...
别动！举起手！
Freeze! Hands up!
等等...我是——
Wait... I'm—
闭嘴！你有权保持沉默！
Shut up! You have the right to remain silent!
我没有杀他！我是——
I didn't kill him! I was—
被栽赃？都这么说。
Framed? They all say that.
等一下！《艾斯弗德先驱报》记者Emma O'Malley。我拍到有人从大门离开。
Wait! Emma O'Malley, Ashford Herald. I photographed someone leaving through the back door.
记者？滚出去！这是犯罪现场！
A reporter? Get out! This is a crime scene!
我有照片证据，警探。
I have photographic evidence, Detective.
而且一个称职的警探不会在看到现场后的第一反应就是'逮捕他'，而不是'封锁现场、寻找线索'。
A competent detective doesn't arrest the first person they see. They secure the scene and look for clues.
小姑娘，别多管闲事。
Little girl, don't stick your nose where it doesn't belong.
Thomas Morrison警探，艾斯弗德警局刑侦科，二十年警龄。您曾经处理过几起争议案件。
Detective Thomas Morrison, Ashford PD Criminal Investigation, twenty years on the force. You've handled several controversial cases.
如果您现在草率逮捕嫌疑人，明天《先驱报》头版就会很精彩。
If you make a hasty arrest now, tomorrow's Herald front page will be quite something.
当然，如果您按规矩办事，给这位私家侦探一个证明清白的机会，那么过去的事就是过去的事。
If you follow procedure and give this detective a chance to prove his innocence, the past stays in the past.
毕竟，真正的凶手还在逃。
After all, the real killer is still out there.
听着，Brennan。72小时。找不到真凶，我让你在监狱里烂掉。
Listen, Brennan. 72 hours. Don't find the real killer, and I'll let you rot in prison.
还有你。妨碍司法，够你受的。
And you. Obstruction of justice. That'll be enough to deal with you.
谁？...楼下出了大事...有点可怕...
Who?... Something big happened downstairs... It's a bit scary...
私家侦探，Zack Brennan。你是？
Private detective, Zack Brennan. And you are?
我...我是Tommy，酒吧经理...您就是那个...在Webb先生房间里被发现的...
I... I'm Tommy, the bar manager... You're the one... who was found in Mr. Webb's room...
好。我问你几个问题。
Alright. Let me ask you a few questions.
当然...当然...我能帮上什么忙...
Of course... of course... how can I help...
Webb是做什么生意的？
What business did Webb run?
您...您不知道？蓝月亮是艾斯弗德南区最大的酒吧...Webb先生是老板...
You... you don't know? Blue Moon is the biggest bar in Ashford's South Side... Mr. Webb was the owner...
只是酒吧？
Just a bar?
Webb先生...对我们这些下面干活的人...还算不错...工钱准时，从不拖欠...
Mr. Webb... was pretty good to us workers... paid on time, never late...
但是...他做事...很有手段。有些来找他的人...进去时趾高气扬，出来时...脸都是白的...
But... he had... his ways of doing things. Some people who came to see him... walked in arrogant, walked out... pale as a ghost...
我...我不是说他是坏人！只是...这一行...您懂的...我只管账目，其他的...不该看的我不看...
I... I'm not saying he was a bad person! It's just... this business... you know... I only handle the books, the rest... I don't see what I shouldn't see...
今晚你在哪儿？
Where were you tonight?
在...在办公室！一直在办公室！您看，这些都是今晚整理的账本...
In... in the office! I was in the office the whole time! Look, these are the ledgers I organized tonight...
一个人？
Alone?
是...是的...这两天总是...很忙...我从十点就开始...一直到...到现在...
Yes... yes... These past two days are always... busy... I started at ten... all the way to... to now...
所以没人能证明？
So no one can vouch for you?
账本可以证明！您看这些数字...这些都是...都是需要时间的...我真的一直在这儿...
The ledgers can prove it! Look at these numbers... these all... take time... I really was here the whole time...
昨晚有什么异常？
Anything unusual last night?
听到了...枪声...很响，吓了我一跳...
I heard it... a gunshot... very loud, it startled me...
几点？准确时间。
What time? Exact time.
11点30分。我看了表...当时正在核对这笔账...听到枪声我就...就看了一眼表...
11:30. I looked at my watch... I was checking this account at the time... when I heard the gunshot, I... I just glanced at my watch...
只有一声？
Just one shot?
对...就一声...平时要是有麻烦...都是一连串的枪声，很乱，砰砰砰...像放鞭炮一样。但那时候...就一声。
Yes... just one shot... Usually when there's trouble... it's a barrage of gunshots, very chaotic, bang bang bang... like firecrackers. But then... Just one shot.
处决式的。
Execution style.
我...我不想往那个方向想...但是...是的...像是有人很清楚自己在做什么...
I... I didn't want to think in that direction... but... yes... like someone knew exactly what they were doing...
账本里有什么不对劲的地方吗？
Anything off in those books?
我...我只是整理数字...从来不多问...
I... I just organize the numbers... never ask questions...
聪明。但Webb死了，这些账本可能比你想的更重要。
Smart. But Webb is dead, those books might be more important than you think.
您...您是什么意思？
What... what do you mean?
如果想起什么，来找我。
If you remember anything, come find me.
我...我会的...
I... I will...
账本能证明你在这儿，但证明不了你没离开过。
The ledgers prove you were here, but not that you never left.
我...我真的没有...您可以问别人...
I... I really didn't... you can ask others...
问谁？你说一个人在这儿。
Ask who? You said you were alone here.
我...楼下有人...酒保...他们可能看到我...
I... there are people downstairs... bartenders... they might have seen me...
如果有人能证明，最好快点找到。
If someone can vouch for you, better find them fast.
我...我明白...
I... I understand...
Tommy，Webb死了。
Tommy, Webb is dead.
我...我知道...
I... I know...
Webb死了，局面变了。你知道什么，现在最好说出来。
Webb is dead, things have changed. Whatever you know, better say it now.
我...我真的不知道更多了！Webb先生对我不错，给的工钱也公道...我...我不想卷进这些事...求您了...
I... I really don't know more! Mr. Webb was good to me, paid me fairly... I... I don't want to get involved in these things... please...
如果你想起什么，来找我。
If you remember anything, come find me.
好的，好的，Brennan先生...如果我想起什么...一定...一定告诉您...
Yes, yes, Mr. Brennan... if I remember anything... I will... I will definitely tell you...
Tommy，别太留意。太留意的人，在这行活不长。
Tommy, don't pay too much attention. People who pay too much attention don't last long in this business.
我...我明白...
I... I understand...
具体负责什么？
What exactly do you handle?
收...收账，排班...还有一些杂事...Webb先生让我做什么，我就做什么...
Col...collecting payments, scheduling shifts... and some odd jobs... Whatever Mr. Webb asked me to do, I did...
又是您？我...我刚才都说过了...
You again? I... I already told you everything...
我还有几个问题想再确认一下。
I have a few more questions to confirm.
好...好的...您请问吧...我能帮上什么忙...
O-okay... please ask... how can I help...
暂时没有了。
That's all for now.
好...好的，Brennan先生...如果还有什么需要...您随时来找我...
O-okay, Mr. Brennan... if you need anything else... feel free to come find me...
但为什么？为什么Whale要这么做？为什么不直接杀了你？
But why? Why does Whale do this? Why not just kill you directly?
因为直接杀我太引人注目。我是私家侦探，如果我也死了，会引发更大的调查。
Because killing me directly is too conspicuous. I'm a private detective; if I die too, it triggers a bigger investigation.
所以这是一种...困住你的方式？
So this is a way... to trap you?
而且Webb得罪了某个危险的人。危险到他需要提前准备一个'保险侦探'。
And Webb had crossed someone dangerous. Dangerous enough to require an 'insurance detective' prepared in advance.
'保险侦探'...就是你。Webb约你去酒吧，不是要给你案子，而是把你当成保险。
'Insurance detective'... that's you. Webb invited you to the bar not to give you a case, but to use you as insurance.
没错。那天Webb给我打电话，语气很急。让我晚上11点去他的会客室见他。但我迟到了......我不记得迟到了多久。
Correct. Webb called me that day, sounding urgent. Told me to meet him in his parlor at 11 PM. But I was late... I don't remember how long.
所以他利用了你的职业操守。
So he exploited your professional integrity.
我被一个死人利用了。很讽刺，对吧？
I was used by a dead man. Ironic, isn't it?
你很生气。
You are angry.
我很愤怒，但也很好奇。为什么是我？艾斯弗德那么多侦探，为什么偏偏选一个名不见经传的私家侦探？
I am furious, but also curious. Why me? Of all the detectives in Ashford, why choose an obscure private eye?
但你还在继续调查。
But you are still investigating.
因为我想知道真相。Webb到底在勒索谁？危险到他需要雇一个'保险侦探'？
Because I want the truth. Who was Webb blackmailing? Someone dangerous enough to require an 'insurance detective'?
这就是我们接下来要查的。Webb的勒索生意。
That's what we need to investigate next. Webb's blackmail business.
找出他在勒索谁，就能找出Whale是谁，找出真凶是谁。
Find out who he was blackmailing, and we find Whale, and the real killer.
从哪里开始？
Where do we start?
回去以前的蓝月亮看看。随便转转，问问那些员工。
Go back to the Blue Moon. Look around, ask the staff.
Brennan，Webb已经死了。你还在为他工作。
Brennan, Webb is dead. You are still working for him.
不。我不是为他工作。我是为我自己。我要证明我的清白，找出真凶。
No. I'm not working for him. I'm working for myself. To prove my innocence, find the killer.
落空？
Fail?
Webb以为我会像他的工具一样，乖乖查案。但我不是。
Webb thought I'd be his tool, dutifully investigating. But I'm not.
你真的很恨他。
You really hate him.
我恨所有利用别人的人。Webb，Whale，Morrison...这个城市就是靠这个运转的。但至少这一次，我要让他们付出代价。
I hate everyone who uses others. Webb, Whale, Morrison... this city runs on it. But this time, I'll make them pay.
那我们现在就去蓝月亮？
So we go to the Blue Moon now?
立刻。
Immediately.
不休息休息吗？
Don't you want to rest?
我们只有三天。
We only have three days.
好吧。但之后你得休息。你看起来快倒了。
Fine. But afterwards you need to rest. You look ready to collapse.
我撑得住。走吧。
I'll hold up. Let's go.
Brennan，等等。
Brennan, wait.
什么？
What?
无论Webb怎么利用你，至少他选对了人。你会找出真相的。
However Webb used you, at least he picked the right person. You will find the truth.
也许。或者我只是个被所有人利用的傻子。
Maybe. Or I'm just a fool used by everyone.
你不是傻子。你只是...太坚持了。
You are not a fool. You just... persist too much.
坚持。这是夸奖还是讽刺？
Persist. Is that a compliment or sarcasm?
两者都是。走吧，侦探先生。让我们去找出Webb到底在勒索谁。
Both. Let's go, Mr. Detective. Let's find out who Webb was blackmailing.
麻烦的记者。
Troublesome reporter.
您说什么？
What did you say?
没什么。走吧。
Nothing. Let's go.
Whale更聪明——让我被当成凶手，这样我就被困在案子里，无法继续调查其他事情。
Whale is smarter—getting me framed as the killer keeps me trapped in the case, unable to investigate anything else.
等我到的时候，已经太晚了。
By the time I arrived, it was too late.
Webb把我当工具，Whale把我当目标......所有人都在利用我。
Webb used me as a tool, Whale targets me... everyone is using me.
Webb在那边待的时间比家里还长，总有人知道点什么。
Webb spent more time there than at home, someone must know something.
顺便...让Webb的算计落空。
And... make Webb's scheme fail.
我会找出真相，然后把所有人都拉下水。包括他。
I'll find the truth, and drag everyone down. Including him.
而且我反正也睡不着。走吧。
And I can't sleep anyway. Let's go.
愤怒是浪费精力。我只是在计算......计算他们欠我的账。
Anger is a waste of energy. I'm just calculating... calculating the debt they owe me.
生气？不。我只是在欣赏这个完美的陷阱。Webb，Whale... 每个人都扮演着自己的角色。真是一出好戏。
Angry? No. I'm admiring this perfect trap. Webb, Whale... everyone playing their role. Quite a show.
不，我不为任何人工作。我只是无法忍受一个没有结局的故事。这算是侦探的坏毛病。
No, I work for no one. I just can't stand a story without an ending. Call it a detective's bad habit.
他在坟墓里看着我呢。如果我不查到底，我就输了。我不会让他赢到最后。
He's watching me from the grave. If I don't dig to the bottom, I lose to a dead man. I won't let him have the last laugh.
坏毛病？
Bad habit?
让他赢？
Let him win?
Vivian说11点10分最后一曲结束，大家就陆陆续续离开了。
Vivian said the last song ended at 11:10, and people started leaving.
所以凶手大概率还是酒吧里面的那些人。
So the killer is likely one of the people inside the bar.
Tommy，Rosa，Vivian……还有哪些没见过的厨房员工们。
Tommy, Rosa, Vivian... and whatever kitchen staff we haven't seen.
也许我就是个傻子。一个不撞南墙不回头的傻子。
Maybe I am a fool. A fool who doesn't turn back until he hits the wall.
在这个行业，'坚持'通常意味着'麻烦'。看来我是个大麻烦。
In this business, 'persist' usually means 'trouble'. Guess I'm just big trouble walking.
Rosa，我需要再问你几个问题。
Rosa, I need to ask you a few more questions.
Brennan先生...我...我会尽力回答的...您...您需要坐下吗？这里有椅子...虽然不太干净...
Mr. Brennan... I... I will try my best to answer... Do... do you need to sit? There is a chair... though not very clean...
Morrison那天做了什么？
What did Morrison do that night?
那天晚上...我...我之前已经告诉过您了...我看到Morrison警官把您拖进了会客室...
That night... I... I already told you before... I saw Officer Morrison drag you into the guest room...
我...我害怕极了...所以躲到了储藏室...大概十二点半吧...中间有一段时间我一直躲着，什么都不敢看...
I... I was terrified... so I hid in the storage room... around half past twelve... I hid there for a while, didn't dare to look at anything...
后来...后来听到外面有动静，其他员工也来了...我才和他们一起出来...那时候Morrison警官已经叫来了其他警察...
Later... later I heard noises outside, other staff came... I came out with them... by then Officer Morrison had called other police...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 你后来看到他在现场做了什么？

Words: 我...我和其他员工站在门口看...Morrison警官在房间里走来走去...看了看地上的...看了看Webb先生...还有您...
I... I stood at the door with other staff watching... Officer Morrison walked around the room... looked at the ground... looked at Mr. Webb... and you...
他...他蹲下来看了看...但没有碰任何东西...然后就...就站起来，出来了...
He... he squatted down and looked... but didn't touch anything... then just... stood up and came out...
从他被指证，到他离开会客室——他用过任何工具吗？尺子、相机、手套？
From when he was accused to when he left the guest room—did he use any tools? Rulers, cameras, gloves?
没有...什么都没有...他空着手进去，空着手出来...我看得很清楚，因为我一直盯着他的手...他的手一直都是空的...
No... nothing at all... he went in empty-handed, came out empty-handed... I saw clearly, because I kept staring at his hands... his hands were always empty...
他早就知道现场是什么样的。因为是他布置的。
He already knew what the scene looked like. Because he staged it.
是...是的...他之前...之前就已经...因为我...对不起...Brennan先生...
Yes... yes... he before... already... because I... I'm sorry... Mr. Brennan...
Rosa，不是你的错。Morrison是警察，但我会揭穿他。你照顾好你女儿就行。
Rosa, it's not your fault. Morrison is police, but I will expose him. You just take care of your daughter.
谢谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Thank you... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
告诉你女儿，她是个勇敢的女孩。她的妈妈也是。
Tell your daughter she's a brave girl. Her mother is as well.
你女儿怎么样了？
How is your daughter?
好...好多了...上次的药很管用...医生说药物有效，让我继续给她用...
Good... much better... the medicine last time worked well... the doctor said it's effective...
那就好。Morrison还没发现你告诉我的事。你和你女儿都很安全。
That's good. Morrison hasn't discovered what you told me. You and your daughter are safe.
谢谢您...真的谢谢您，Brennan先生...如果不是您...我...我真的不知道该怎么办...
Thank you... thank you so much, Mr. Brennan... if it weren't for you... I... I really don't know what I would do...
照顾好她。
Take care of her.
今天就到这。照顾好你女儿。
That's all for today. Take care of your daughter.
谢谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Thank you... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都已经告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You... you're here again? I... I already told you everything before...
我还有几个问题想再确认一下。
I have a few more questions to confirm.
好...好的...您请问吧...
O... okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。照顾好你女儿。
No more questions. Take care of your daughter.
谢谢您...Brennan先生...
Thank you... Mr. Brennan...
Morrison？你在这做什么？
Morrison? What are you doing here?
在等警员们来封锁现场。
Waiting for officers to seal the scene.
又见面了，私家侦探。
Met you again, private eye.
我以为不管是哪个牢房，都该教会你什么是'规矩'了。
Thought the cell would've taught you 'rules'.
规矩我懂。但真相似乎还没查清。
I know rules. But truth isn't clear.
真相？真相就是这里是封锁区域。
Truth? Truth is this is a sealed area.
离会客室远点，尤其是那扇门。别逼我再把你铐起来。
Stay away from the receiving room, especially that door. Don't make me cuff you again.
你只是这一片的巡警，Morrison。
You're just a beat cop for this area, Morrison.
什么意思？
What's that supposed to mean?
这种大案子通常由刑侦科接手。你却守在门口充当指挥官...这不合规矩吧？
Homicide usually goes to Criminal Investigation. Yet here you are, acting commander... that's against protocol, isn't it?
注意你的态度，Brennan！
Watch it, Brennan!
我是这片街区的治安负责人，蓝月亮酒吧属于我的管辖范围。
I'm the officer in charge of this beat, Blue Moon is under my jurisdiction.
在我把案子移交之前，这里我说了算。懂了吗？
Until I hand it over, what I say goes. Got it?
记住，我说的话就是规矩。
Remember, what I say is the rule.
现在，离开我的现场。
Now, get off my scene.
警方的反应速度真快。
Police reaction is fast.
这是我们的效率。
That's our efficiency.
我前脚刚发现尸体，你后脚就到。简直就像...你就在门口等着一样。
I find the body, you arrive instantly. Like... you were waiting at the door.
别想套我的话！
Don't try to trap me!
我是在家接到匿名线报后，按照流程立刻赶往现场的。
I received an anonymous tip at home and followed procedure to rush here immediately.
再多嘴，我就以妨碍公务把你拷回去。
One more word, and I'll cuff you for obstruction of justice.
别以为你能挖出什么。
Don't think you can dig up anything.
滚，趁我还没改变主意。
Get lost, before I change my mind.
Webb雇我的时候，只说是一些'琐事'。
Webb hired me for 'trivia'.
所以呢？
So?
既然是你的辖区......你和Webb平时没少打交道吧？
Since it's your jurisdiction... you and Webb must have dealt with each other a lot, right?
他也配？
He deserves?
我和Webb仅限于公事公办的工作关系，他的生意细节我从不过问。
My relationship with Webb was strictly business, I never asked about his business details.
我只负责维持治安。至于他死在哪个阴沟里，我才懒得管。
I only keep the peace. Don't care which gutter he dies in.
Webb的事跟我没关系。
Webb's business has nothing to do with me.
你最好也别把我牵扯进去。滚。
You'd better not drag me into this. Get lost.
这么早就开始工作了，Tommy？
Starting work so early, Tommy?
Brennan先生？早...早上好。我正在核对昨晚的账目。您...您找我有事吗？
Mr. Brennan? Good... good morning. I'm checking last night's accounts. You... do you need something?
关于Morrison。
About Morrison.
Morrison警探？我...我不太...他是警察...您知道的...我们...我们不好多问...
Detective Morrison? I... I don't really... he's police... you know... we... we shouldn't ask too much...

Parameters.ParameterStr: Webb付你工资，Morrison栽赃我，你帮谁？
如果你想活命，最好想清楚。

Words: Webb付你工资，Morrison栽赃我。你选择帮谁？
Webb pays you, Morrison frames me. Who do you choose to help?
如果你想活命，最好想清楚站在哪一边。Webb已经死了。
If you want to live, better think clearly which side you're on. Webb is already dead.
每个人都有选择，Tommy。别说你没有。
Everyone has a choice, Tommy. Don't say you don't.
Brennan先生，我...我没有选择...Webb先生已经...已经死了...而Morrison警长他...他有权力...
Mr. Brennan, I... I have no choice... Mr. Webb is already... already dead... and Sergeant Morrison he... he has power...
我只是负责账目和日常...其他的...真的不关我的事...
I just handle accounts and daily operations... the rest... really isn't my business...
'不该问的不问'。艾斯弗德的生存法则？
'Don't ask what you shouldn't'. Ashford survival rule?
这行的规矩...您应该...应该理解...我...我不想卷进这些事...
Rules of the trade... you should... should understand... I... I don't want to get involved in this...
Webb死了，规矩也变了，Tommy。现在不是'不该问的不问'，而是'知道什么说什么'。
Webb is dead, the rules changed, Tommy. Now it's not 'don't ask', it's 'say what you know'.
你和Morrison打过交道吗？
Have you dealt with Morrison before?
我...偶尔...去过他家几次...
I... occasionally... been to his place a few times...
去他家干嘛？
Why go to his place?
送...送东西...您知道的，这一行做生意，给警察送点东西是常事...
Deliver... deliver things... you know, in this business, giving cops a little something is normal...
多远？
How far?
从他家到这里...大概十五分钟吧...那次快下班了，我开得挺快...走最近的路...最多十五分钟...
From his place to here... about fifteen minutes... I was off work soon, drove pretty fast... shortest route... at most fifteen minutes...
十五分钟。你确定？
Fifteen minutes. You sure?
确定...我记得很清楚...那次我还看了表...真的只要十五分钟...
Sure... I remember clearly... I checked my watch that time... really only fifteen minutes...
如果Morrison问起，就说我只问了距离。这不算什么。
If Morrison asks, say I only asked about the distance. That's nothing.
还有，你也小心。Morrison不是好惹的。
Also, you be careful too. Morrison isn't one to mess with.
谢谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Thank you... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
Webb最近有什么异常？
Anything unusual with Webb lately?
我...我不太清楚...Webb先生很少和我说那些事...我就管账目...
I... I don't really know... Mr. Webb rarely told me about those things... I just handle the accounts...
行吧。
Alright.
继续忙你的。
Keep at it.
好...好的，Brennan先生...
O... okay, Mr. Brennan...
Brennan先生？您...您又来了？我...我说的够多了吧...
Mr. Brennan? You... you're here again? I... I've said enough, haven't I...
有些事我想再确认一下。
There are some things I want to confirm again.
好...好的...您请说...
O... okay... please go ahead...
没有其他问题了。小心点，Tommy。
No more questions. Be careful, Tommy.
谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Th... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
Vivian女士，我需要问您几个问题。
Ms. Vivian, I need to ask you a few questions.
把门关上。我不接受任何采访。
Close the door. I don't accept any interviews.
我不是记者。我是Zack Brennan——
I'm not a reporter. I'm Zack Brennan—
我知道你是谁。那个在Webb办公室醒来的'私家侦探'。Morrison警长似乎认为你就是凶手。
I know who you are. The 'private eye' who woke up in Webb's office. Sergeant Morrison seems to think you're the killer.
Morrison说了很多话。但如果我真是凶手，我现在应该在牢里，而不是站在这里。
Morrison says a lot. But if I were really the killer, I'd be in jail, not standing here.
那只是因为那个小记者救了你。Brennan先生，我很忙，如果没有别的事——
That's only because that little reporter saved you. Mr. Brennan, I'm busy, if there's nothing else—
Morrison警探到达现场的时间。凌晨几点？
Detective Morrison's arrival time at the scene. What time in the morning?
12点。Morrison匆匆赶到，那会儿我正在准备离开。警笛声打断了我的计划。
12:00. Morrison arrived in a hurry, just as I was preparing to leave. Sirens interrupted my plans.
他看起来怎么样？着急吗？
How did he look? Anxious?
着急？不...我会说他显得...胸有成竹。就像一个演员走上舞台，早已背熟了台词。
Anxious? No... I'd say he looked... confident. Like an actor stepping on stage, lines already memorized.
谢谢你的配合。
Thank you for your cooperation.
我并非在'配合'您的调查，Brennan先生。我只是回答了几个对我而言无关紧要的问题。
I am not 'cooperating' with your investigation, Mr. Brennan. I merely answered a few trivia questions of no consequence to me.
现在，如果您真想找出凶手，我建议您把时间花在那些真正可疑的人身上——而不是打扰一个正在为演出做准备的演员。
Now, if you really want to find the killer, I suggest you spend time on those actually suspicious—not disturbing an actress preparing for a show.
打扰了，Vivian女士。
Sorry to disturb, Ms. Vivian.
关门时请轻一点。还有，Brennan先生——下次敲门之前，请先确认对方是否方便接待。这是基本的礼貌。
Close the door gently. Also, Mr. Brennan—next time check if one is available before knocking. That's basic courtesy.
你和Webb的关系？你们之间似乎不只是老板和员工。
Your relationship with Webb? Seems like more than just boss and employee.
Brennan先生，你根本不了解我和Webb的关系。我们之间...不是随便就能概括的。但这和你找凶手没关系。
Mr. Brennan, you don't understand my relationship with Webb at all. It's... not easily summarized. But it has nothing to do with finding the killer.
现在，如果您真想找出凶手，我建议您把时间花在那些真正可疑的人身上——而不是打扰一个正在为演出做准备的演员。
Now, if you really want to find the killer, I suggest you spend time on those actually suspicious—not disturbing an actress preparing for a show.

Close the door gently. Also, Mr. Brennan—next time check if one is available before knocking. That's basic courtesy.
当天这里有什么活动？
What event was held here that day?
一个酒会。我10点上台，11点10分最后一曲结束，大家就陆陆续续离开了。
A cocktail party. I started at 10, the last song ended at 11:10, and people started leaving.
什么时候结束营业？
When does it close?
每晚。下次您可以早点来，Brennan先生。
Every night. Next time come earlier, Mr. Brennan.
谢谢你的配合。
Thank you for your cooperation.
我并非在'配合'您的调查，Brennan先生。我只是回答了几个对我而言无关紧要的问题。
I am not 'cooperating' with your investigation, Mr. Brennan. I merely answered a few trivia questions of no consequence to me.
现在，如果您真想找出凶手，我建议您把时间花在那些真正可疑的人身上——而不是打扰一个正在为演出做准备的演员。
Now, if you really want to find the killer, I suggest you spend time on those actually suspicious—not disturbing an actress preparing for a show.

Sorry to disturb, Ms. Vivian.
