Close the door gently. Also, Mr. Brennan—next time check if one is available before knocking. That's basic courtesy.
你还想听什么？我没什么好说的了。
What else do you want to hear? I have nothing more to say.
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details.
快点问吧，我时间宝贵。
Ask quickly, my time is precious.
没有其他问题了。打扰了。
No more questions. Sorry to bother you.
下次记得先敲门。
Remember to knock next time.
Morrison太太？《艾斯弗德先驱报》。关于昨晚蓝月亮的案子，我们想做一篇关于警探家属的报道。
Mrs. Morrison? Ashford Herald. Regarding the Blue Moon case last night, we'd like to do a report on detective families.
记者？请坐。Thomas不在家，他在警局处理案件。这位是...？
Reporters? Please sit. Thomas isn't home, he's handling cases at the station. This is...?
哦，这是我的摄影助理Brennan。负责拍照和记录。
Oh, this is my photography assistant Brennan. Responsible for photos and records.
哦，这个角度不错。请坐。我去给你们倒咖啡。
Oh, that's a good angle. Please sit. I'll pour you some coffee.
咖啡。糖罐在这儿。
Coffee. Sugar is here.
谢谢您，Morrison太太。
Thank you, Mrs. Morrison.
谢谢。
Thank you.
您...真喜欢甜的。Thomas也喜欢加糖，不过没这么多。
You... really like sweets. Thomas also likes adding sugar, but not this much.
Morrison太太，我们想让读者了解警察工作对家庭的影响。很多人只看到制服和警徽，但不知道每个深夜电话背后，还有一个担心丈夫安危的妻子。

Mrs. Morrison, we want readers to understand the impact of police work on families. Many only see the uniform and badge, but don't know behind every late-night call, there's a wife worrying about her husband's safety.
您说得...太对了。Thomas做了二十年警察，我已经习惯了他半夜接电话就走。
You're... exactly right. Thomas has been a policeman for twenty years, I'm used to him taking calls at midnight and leaving.
昨晚也是这样吗？
Was it like that last night too?
对。11点30分，电话响了，Thomas说有紧急案件，立刻就出门了。和往常一样。
Yes. 11:30, the phone rang, Thomas said there was an emergency case, and left immediately. Just like always.
Thomas从来不紧张。二十年的老警察了，什么场面没见过。他很专业。
Thomas is never nervous. Twenty years as a veteran cop, he's seen it all. He's very professional.
这很好...读者会喜欢这种细节。您真是个支持丈夫的好妻子，Morrison太太。
That's good... readers will love these details. You really are a supportive wife, Mrs. Morrison.
谢谢。我只是做我该做的。你们不像其他记者那么...咄咄逼人。这很好。
Thank you. I'm just doing what I should. You aren't as... aggressive as other reporters. That's good.
Morrison太太，我想让读者看到警探真实的工作环境。能让我们看看Morrison警探的书房吗？Brennan可以拍几张照片。
Mrs. Morrison, I want readers to see the detective's real working environment. Can we see Detective Morrison's study? Brennan can take a few photos.
书房？Thomas的书房都是案件资料...我不太确定他会不会希望外人看到。
Study? Thomas's study is full of case files... I'm not sure if he would want outsiders to see it.
我理解您的顾虑。我们不会看具体内容，只是拍几张环境照片——书桌、书架这些。展现警探的工作状态。
I understand your concern. We won't look at specific content, just take some environmental photos—desk, bookshelf. Show the detective's working state.
就像办公室的环境照。不会触碰任何文件。
Just like office environment shots. Won't touch any documents.
这个...我得先问问Thomas。他对工作资料管得很严。毕竟是警察的规矩。
This... I have to ask Thomas first. He's very strict about work materials. Police rules, after all.
其实这正说明Morrison警探的专业和谨慎。这种细节正是读者想看到的——一个严谨、负责的警探形象。
Actually, this just shows Detective Morrison's professionalism and caution. This detail is exactly what readers want to see—a rigorous, responsible detective image.
Thomas确实一向很认真。他做了二十年警察，一直都很专业。从不出错。
Thomas is indeed always serious. He's been a policeman for twenty years, always professional. Never makes mistakes.
二十年...那他一定见过很多案子。
Twenty years... then he must have seen many cases.
太多了。尤其最近，他工作很忙。不过Thomas能处理好，他一直都很有经验。
Too many. Especially recently, he's very busy. But Thomas can handle it, he's always been experienced.
像Morrison警探这样尽职的警察，书房里一定都是正常的工作资料吧？
For a dedicated policeman like Detective Morrison, the study must be full of normal work materials, right?
当然。Thomas是个好警察，他的书房除了案卷就是案卷。有时候我进去打扫，连落脚的地方都没有。他总说'警察的工作就是这样'。
Of course. Thomas is a good cop, his study is nothing but case files. Sometimes when I go in to clean, there's no place to step. He always says 'That's police work'.
那您让我们看一下，也能让读者更了解警察的工作状态。这对Morrison警探的形象也是个好宣传。
Then let us have a look, it will let readers understand the police working state better. This is also good publicity for Detective Morrison's image.
我们会很快。十分钟。
We'll be quick. Ten minutes.
你们说得对...而且Thomas最近确实需要一些正面报道。好吧。走廊尽头，左边第一间。不过那些文件不要动。Thomas回来如果发现有人动过，会不高兴的。

You're right... and Thomas does need some positive coverage recently. Alright. End of the hallway, first door on the left. But don't touch those files. If Thomas finds out someone touched them, he won't be happy.
谢谢您，Morrison太太。我们会注意的。
Thank you, Mrs. Morrison. We will be careful.
我去准备相机。
I'll prepare the camera.
我去给你们准备茶点。顺便...如果你们拍照的话，记得把画面拍得整洁一些。Thomas虽然工作忙，但不希望别人觉得他邋遢。
I'll prepare some tea and snacks for you. By the way... if you take photos, remember to make the shot look tidy. Thomas is busy, but he doesn't want people to think he's sloppy.
当然。我们理解。
Of course. We understand.
Thomas知道了一定会...满意的。毕竟，警察家庭也需要社会的理解。
Thomas will certainly be... satisfied when he knows. After all, police families also need social understanding.
十分钟。你在这里盯着。
Ten minutes. You watch here.
小心。
Be careful.
生活已经够苦了，咖啡得甜一点。
Life is bitter enough, coffee needs to be sweeter.
它能让我保持清醒。干这行需要时刻睁着眼睛。
It keeps me awake. This job requires keeping eyes open at all times.
Thomas在警局也喝很多咖啡。他说如果不喝，根本撑不过那些夜班。
Thomas also drinks a lot of coffee at the station. He says he can't make it through the night shifts without it.
这是大脑的燃料。思考是很消耗能量的。
It's brain fuel. Thinking consumes a lot of energy.
我想也是。警探们总是有想不完的事情。
I suppose so. Detectives always have endless things to think about.
他看起来紧张吗？
Did he look nervous?
他说谁打来的吗？
Did he say who called?
没说。只说很紧急，是警局打来的。
He didn't say. Just said it was urgent, called from the station.
他带枪了吗？
Did he take his gun?
他总是带着。他说这是也是身体的一部分。
He always takes it. He says it's part of his body too.
Tommy供了。Webb用古董做档案代号，勒索那些'体面人'。
Tommy confessed. Webb used antiques as file codes to blackmail those 'respectable people'.
Thompson议员、Coleman银行家……禁酒令的布道者，半夜来这里买私酒。Webb只不过把他们的伪装收集得更精致些。
Senator Thompson, Banker Coleman... preachers of Prohibition, buying bootleg liquor here at midnight. Webb just collected their disguises more exquisitely.
你就一点不震惊？
Are you not shocked at all?
在Kirkland的时候我天天替他们洗白。他们白天在教堂高喊道德，晚上在地下赌场输得精光。Webb只是把这门生意做得更系统化——他有流程表。
At Kirkland, I whitewashed them every day. By day they shout morals in church, by night they lose everything in underground casinos. Webb just systematized the business—he had flowcharts.
你总是这样，好像什么都见过。
You're always like this, as if you've seen everything.
因为这城市只信两样东西——钱和权力。正义？那是穷人讲给自己听的睡前故事。
Because this city only believes in two things—money and power. Justice? That's a bedtime story poor people tell themselves.
可你还在追案子。
But you're still chasing the case.
除了查案我也不知道自己能干什么。总得找点事做，不然真要发疯。
I don't know what else I can do besides investigating. Have to do something, or I'd really go mad.
我懂。我做记者也是这样。总觉得如果不做点什么，我的父母就白死了。
I understand. Being a reporter is like that for me too. I feel if I don't do something, my parents died for nothing.
你父母？
Your parents?
1912年的工程意外，钢架倒塌...他们当场就...所以我才报道工人安全问题。
Engineering accident in 1912, steel frame collapsed... they died instantly... That's why I report on worker safety.
...对不起。
...I'm sorry.
不用。已经十六年了。我只是不想再有人重复同样的悲剧。
Don't be. It's been sixteen years. I just don't want anyone to repeat the same tragedy.
所以你'在乎真相'。
So you 'care about the truth'.
你不也是吗？不然为什么还在查案？
Aren't you too? Otherwise, why keep investigating?
也许只是固执。或者...不知道还能做什么。
Maybe just stubbornness. Or... don't know what else to do.
好了，说正事。Webb办公室里有本日记——上面有个名字，'Whale'，旁边标着'DANGER'。
Anyway, let's get back to business. There's a diary in Webb's office—with a name, 'Whale', marked 'DANGER' next to it.
Whale？
Whale?
一个非常危险的人。我们调查办公室的时候，还接到了恐吓电话，估计是他。
A very dangerous man. We got a threatening call while investigating the office. Probably him.
所以Webb是惹上了不该惹的人...
So Webb messed with the wrong person...
对。Webb知道危险，但还是继续勒索。要么是贪心，要么是...已经没有退路了。
Yes. Webb knew it was dangerous but kept blackmailing. Either greed, or... he had no way out.
所以'Whale'先下手为强——杀了Webb，又找Morrison来擦屁股。
So 'Whale' struck first—killed Webb, then got Morrison to clean up.
还有一件事。Vivian说她曾撞见厨师Jimmy和Webb在激烈争吵，Jimmy警告Webb'你会害死所有人'。
One more thing. Vivian said she witnessed the cook Jimmy arguing fiercely with Webb. Jimmy warned Webb 'you'll get everyone killed'.
一个厨师？他怎么会知道这么多？
A cook? How would he know all this?
正是我想问的。但他今天似乎没来上班，大堂通往厨房的那扇门一直锁着。
Exactly what I want to know. But he seems to be absent today. The door from the lobby to the kitchen has been locked.
所以，明天早上我们直接去Jimmy家里拜访一下。到时候只能随机应变了，看看他能吐出什么来。
So, let's pay a visit to Jimmy's home tomorrow morning. We'll have to play it by ear then, see what he spills.
Jimmy家？你怎么知道地址的？
Jimmy's home? How do you know the address?
刚才在Tommy办公室，我顺便看了一眼工资单。他的地址在上面。
Just now in Tommy's office, I took a glance at the payroll. His address is on it.
偷看工资单？Brennan先生，有没有人说过，您这种'不守规矩'的行事风格...其实挺迷人的？至少比那个只会虚张声势的警探强多了。
Peeking at payrolls? Mr. Brennan, has anyone ever told you that your 'unruly' style is... actually quite charming? At least much better than that bluffing detective.
迷人？要是被警局听到，这叫'妨碍公务'。别夸我了，O'Malley小姐，小心我骄傲得忘记看路。
Charming? If the station heard that, they'd call it 'obstruction of justice'. Stop flattering me, Miss O'Malley, or I might get too proud to watch my step.
晚安，名侦探。明天早上见——别迟到。
Goodnight, Detective. See you tomorrow morning—don't be late.
晚安。路上小心。
Goodnight. Safe home.
Rosa，我需要再问你一些细节。
Rosa, I need to ask you some more details.
Brennan先生，您说。我会尽力回答的...
Mr. Brennan, please ask. I'll try my best to answer...
别紧张。那天晚上11点左右，你在做什么？
Don't be nervous. Around 11 PM that night, what were you doing?
11点...我在清洁走廊和大堂...周五晚上总是最忙的...
11 PM... I was cleaning the hallway and lobby... Friday nights are always busiest...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 这酒吧除了卖酒，还做什么生意？
那天晚上有什么异常吗？

Words: 这酒吧除了卖酒，还做什么生意？
Besides selling drinks, what other business does this bar do?
我...我不知道，先生...Webb先生说过，不要乱看，低头干活就好...
I... I don't know, sir... Mr. Webb said not to look around, just keep your head down and work...
从来没见过什么可疑的？
Never seen anything suspicious?
我不敢看...真的不敢看...我只想保住这份工作养活女儿...
I dare not look... really dare not look... I just want to keep this job to feed my daughter...
我明白了。
I understand.
那天晚上有什么异常吗？
Was there anything unusual that night?
异常？我...我什么都没看到...Webb先生不让我们乱看...
Unusual? I... I didn't see anything... Mr. Webb doesn't let us look around...
听到什么了吗？
Did you hear anything?
听到...11点左右音乐很响，比平时还响...除了音乐我什么都听不到...
Heard... the music was very loud around 11 o'clock, louder than usual... I couldn't hear anything besides the music...
好。谢谢你，Rosa。
Okay. Thank you, Rosa.
Brennan先生...您...您也要小心...
Mr. Brennan... you... be careful too...
你知道Webb雇佣我的事吗？
Do you know about Webb hiring me?
雇佣您？我...我只是个扫地的，先生...这种事我怎么可能知道...
Hiring you? I... I'm just a cleaner, sir... how could I know about such things...
Webb从来没在你面前提过？
Webb never mentioned it in front of you?
没有...Webb先生从来不跟我们这些人说话的...我们见到他都要躲开...
No... Mr. Webb never talks to people like us... we have to stay away when we see him...
行。照顾好你的女儿。
Okay. Take care of your daughter.
今天就到这。照顾好你的女儿，Rosa。
That's all for today. Take care of your daughter, Rosa.
谢谢您，Brennan先生...您也要小心...
Thank you, Mr. Brennan... you be careful too...
Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You... you're here again? I... I told you everything before...
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details again.
好...好的...您请问吧...
O... okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。照顾好你女儿。
No more questions. Take care of your daughter.
谢谢您...Brennan先生...
Thank you... Mr. Brennan...
Tommy，我需要问你一些关于Webb的事。
Tommy, I need to ask you some things about Webb.
Brennan先生...又是您。Webb先生的案子...警察不是在查吗？
Mr. Brennan... again? Mr. Webb's case... aren't the police investigating?
我也在查。
I'm investigating too.
Webb的生意，你了解多少？
How much do you know about Webb's business?
生意？我...我只是个账房先生，负责记账...具体的业务，Webb先生不太...不太让我过问...
Business? I... I'm just a bookkeeper... specific operations, Mr. Webb didn't... didn't let me ask much...
账房先生会记账。那账本上记的是什么？
Bookkeepers keep books. What's recorded in them?
私酒...都是私酒生意。您知道的，禁酒令，但很多人还是想喝酒...Webb先生就做这个...
Bootlegging... all bootlegging business. You know, Prohibition, but many still want to drink... Mr. Webb did this...
只有私酒？
Only bootlegging?
对，只有私酒。Webb先生的生意很简单，从加拿大进货，卖给艾斯弗德的客户...就这样...
Yes, only bootlegging. Mr. Webb's business was simple, imported from Canada, sold to Ashford clients... that's it...
很简单。那为什么你看起来这么紧张？
Very simple. Then why do you look so nervous?
我...我没有紧张...只是...Webb先生死了，大家都很...都很不安...
I... I'm not nervous... just... Mr. Webb is dead, everyone is... very uneasy...
Tommy，做私酒生意不需要这么紧张。除非...他还做了别的。
Tommy, bootlegging doesn't require being this nervous. Unless... he did something else.
没有！真的没有！Webb先生只做私酒生意，绝对没有其他业务！
No! Really no! Mr. Webb only did bootlegging, absolutely no other business!
五年账，你一定记得Webb的主要客户是谁。
Five years of books. You must remember who Webb's main clients are.
客户...很多...酒吧、餐厅、私人买家...我记不清每一个...
Clients... many... bars, restaurants, private buyers... I can't remember everyone...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 这些客户里有'大人物'吗？

Words: 记不清，还是不敢说？
Can't remember, or dare not say?
Brennan先生，我真的只是负责记账...Webb先生的客户都是他自己联系的...我只是...只是记录交易金额和日期...其他的我真的不知道...
Mr. Brennan, I really just keep the books... Mr. Webb contacted his clients himself... I just... just recorded amounts and dates... I really don't know the rest...
如果你想起什么，随时告诉我。
If you remember anything, tell me anytime.
Tommy，Webb不会只做私酒生意。私酒生意不需要'保险侦探'。
Tommy, Webb didn't just do bootlegging. Bootlegging doesn't need an 'insurance detective'.
保险侦探？我...我不知道您在说什么...
Insurance detective? I... I don't know what you're talking about...
Brennan先生，我只是个负责记账的会计...我还有家人要养...我绝不敢参与谋杀...
Mr. Brennan, I'm just an accountant who keeps the books... I have a family to support... I would never dare to be involved in murder...
这些客户里有'大人物'吗？议员？银行家？
Any 'big shots' among these clients? Politicians? Bankers?
我...我不知道名字...在我看来，他们只是账本上的名字，或者一个代号...我不关心他们是谁...
I... I don't know names... to me, they are just names on the ledger, or a code... I don't care who they are...
你在保护谁，Tommy？Webb已经死了。
Who are you protecting, Tommy? Webb is already dead.
没谁！我真的...我只是打份工，养家糊口...我不保护任何人...
No one! I really... I'm just doing a job, feeding my family... I'm not protecting anyone...
没关系。你会知道的。
It's fine. You'll find out.
Webb的办公室，打开了吗？Morrison警官应该给你打了电话的吧。
Webb's office, is it open? Officer Morrison should have called you.
啊...Morrison警官？他...他确实打过电话来...说是有人要检查Webb先生的办公室...
Ah... Officer Morrison? He... he did call... said someone needed to inspect Mr. Webb's office...
那就是我。现在可以打开了吗？
That's me. Can you open it now?
待会儿您就可以进去了...要...要干嘛？
You can go in shortly... What... what for?
看看情况。
Just checking things out.
你知道Webb雇佣我的事吗？
Do you know about Webb hiring me?
知道...我知道Webb先生雇佣了一个以前挺有名的律师，现在当侦探了...具体的情况我不太清楚...Webb先生这样的大人物，请个私家侦探应该不足为奇...
I know... I know Mr. Webb hired a famous former lawyer who's now a detective... I don't know the details... For a big shot like Mr. Webb, hiring a private detective isn't surprising...
他知道自己会死。所以找了我来帮他找出杀他的人是谁。这样的'真相侦探'——你知道吗？
He knew he was going to die. So he hired me to find out who would kill him. This kind of 'truth detective'—did you know about that?
什...什么？他知道自己会死？不...不...我不知道...这...这可能是Webb先生的私事吧...我真的不知道...
Wh... what? He knew he would die? No... no... I don't know... This... this is probably Mr. Webb's private matter... I really don't know...
好。如果你想起什么，随时联系我。
Fine. If you remember anything, contact me anytime.
好...好的...Brennan先生...
O... okay... Mr. Brennan...
今天就到这。
That's all for today.
好...好的...Brennan先生...
O... okay... Mr. Brennan...
Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You... you're here again? I... I told you everything before...
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details again.
好...好的...您请问吧...
O... okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。小心点，Tommy。
No more questions. Be careful, Tommy.
谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Th... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
晚上好，Vivian。希望你还记得我——那个今天凑巧死里逃生的私家侦探。
Good evening, Vivian. Hope you still remember me—the private detective who coincidentally escaped death today.
Morrison今天给Tommy打了电话。听起来很不高兴。
Morrison called Tommy today. Sounded quite unhappy.
那我就不客气了。
Then I won't hold back.
哦？
Oh?
十五分钟的事。能让Morrison亲自打电话来骂人...你还挺有本事。
The fifteen minutes thing. Making Morrison call personally to yell at someone... you've got some nerve.
这算夸奖？
Is that a compliment?
算你运气好，还没被他弄死。问吧，我时间有限。
Consider yourself lucky he hasn't killed you yet. Ask away, my time is limited.
蓝月亮的生意怎么样？看起来挺红火的。
How's Blue Moon's business? Looks quite prosperous.
红火？这里每晚灯红酒绿，客人络绎不绝。但'红火'两个字...要看你怎么定义。
Prosperous? Lights and wine every night, guests coming and going. But 'prosperous'... depends on how you define it.
有什么我该知道的吗？
Anything I should know?
急什么？有些事，知道太多反而危险。
What's the rush? Some things, knowing too much is dangerous.
我习惯了危险。
I'm used to danger.
有耐心的男人不多见。但有些事...还是别知道的好。
Patient men are rare. But some things... better not to know.
听起来挺有意思。
Sounds interesting.
这么说吧——蓝月亮的后台，比舞台上演的戏精彩多了。账本上写的和实际发生的...从来不是一回事。
Let me put it this way—what happens backstage at Blue Moon is more dramatic than what's on stage. What's in the books and what actually happens... never the same thing.
比如？
Such as?
比如...有些客人来这里不是为了听歌。有些钱进来不是为了买酒。Brennan先生，你是聪明人，剩下的自己想。
Such as... some guests come here not to listen to songs. Some money comes in not for drinks. Mr. Brennan, you're smart, figure out the rest yourself.
你倒是会吊人胃口。
You sure know how to keep people guessing.
我是歌手，吊人胃口是本行。想知道更多？去问别人吧。我只负责唱歌。
I'm a singer, keeping people guessing is my job. Want to know more? Ask someone else. I only sing.
行。改天再聊。
Fine. We'll talk another day.
你和Webb是什么关系？
What's your relationship with Webb?
什么关系？老板和员工。他付钱，我唱歌。仅此而已。
What relationship? Boss and employee. He pays, I sing. That's all.
真的只是这样？
Really just that?

Parameters.ParameterStr: 他对你...似乎不只是老板。

Words: 你在胡说什么？
What nonsense are you saying?
那种眼神，不是看老板的眼神。
That look in your eyes, it's not how you look at a boss.
你想多了，Brennan先生。
You're overthinking, Mr. Brennan.
也许吧。但人死了，有些事瞒不住。
Maybe. But when someone dies, some things can't stay hidden.
Vivian，我不是来审判谁的。我只想知道真相。
Vivian, I'm not here to judge anyone. I just want to know the truth.
...是。我们在一起三年了。
...Yes. We've been together for three years.
他不是什么好人，我知道。自负、贪心、满嘴谎话...但他对我...是真的。
He wasn't a good man, I know. Arrogant, greedy, full of lies... but with me... he was real.
我很抱歉。
I'm sorry.
别说抱歉。我不需要同情。
Don't say sorry. I don't need sympathy.
那天晚上...我本来想给他唱一首他最爱的歌。结果...他没能听到。
That night... I was going to sing his favorite song for him. But... he never got to hear it.
你有什么头绪吗？谁会想杀他？
Do you have any leads? Who would want to kill him?
想杀他的人太多了。但真正能下手的...我不知道。但不是我，Brennan先生...我爱他，我怎么可能杀他？我只知道...我再也见不到他了。
Too many people wanted him dead. But who could actually do it... I don't know. But it wasn't me, Mr. Brennan... I loved him, how could I possibly kill him? All I know is... I'll never see him again.
如果想起什么，随时找我。
If you remember anything, come find me anytime.
找出凶手，Brennan先生。为了Webb...也为了我自己能睡个好觉。
Find the killer, Mr. Brennan. For Webb... and so I can sleep at night.
我会的。
I will.
还有一件事。歌舞厅那边上锁了。
One more thing. The Cabaret Hall is locked.
我说过，今天我也没什么心情唱歌，所以让大家都散了。你想进去？
I told you, I'm not in the mood to sing today, so I sent everyone home. You want in?
我想再检查一遍现场。也许有些细节被忽略了。
I want to check the scene again. Maybe some details were missed.
警察已经搜过两遍了，Brennan。不过......如果你坚持的话。
The police have searched it twice, Brennan. But... if you insist.
我会打个电话给看门的，让他给你开门。别在里面给我惹麻烦。
I'll make a call to the doorman, have him open it for you. Don't cause any trouble in there.
我会小心的。谢了，Vivian。
I'll be careful. Thanks, Vivian.
去吧。记得出来时随手带上门。
Go. Remember to close the door when you leave.
今天就到这。打扰了。
That's all for today. Sorry to bother you.
关门时轻一点。
Close the door gently.
你还想听什么？我没什么好说的了。
What else do you want to hear? I have nothing more to say.
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details.
快点问吧，我时间宝贵。
Ask quickly, my time is precious.
没有其他问题了。打扰了。
No more questions. Sorry to bother you.
下次记得先敲门。
Remember to knock next time.
Brennan先生，晚上好。你翻了不该看的东西。
Good evening, Mr. Brennan. You've been looking at things you shouldn't.
你是谁？
Who are you?
Webb是个聪明人，可惜不够聪明。我建议你比他更识相一点。
Webb was a smart man, but not smart enough. I suggest you be wiser than him.
威胁？我听过太多了。
Threats? I've heard plenty.
这不是威胁，Brennan先生。这是忠告。放下这件事，回到你的小公寓，继续接那些婚外情调查。
This isn't a threat, Mr. Brennan. It's advice. Drop this, go back to your little apartment, keep taking those adultery cases.
Webb是你杀的？
Did you kill Webb?
我从不亲自动手，Brennan先生。我只是...提供机会。总有人愿意接受。
I never get my hands dirty, Mr. Brennan. I just... provide opportunities. Someone always takes them.
你怎么知道我在这里？
How do you know I'm here?
我的眼睛无处不在。我擅长抹掉真相——也擅长抹掉追查真相的人。
My eyes are everywhere. I'm good at erasing the truth—and even better at erasing those who chase it.
对了，你母亲住在Maple Street 42号，对吧？那条街晚上很安静。
By the way, your mother lives at 42 Maple Street, right? That street is very quiet at night.
别扯上我母亲！
Leave my mother out of this!
她是个好筹码。希望你能做出正确的选择。
She's a good bargaining chip. I hope you'll make the right choice.
你想怎样？
What do you want?
很简单。停止调查。忘掉Webb，忘掉那本日记，忘掉你在这里看到的一切。
Simple. Stop investigating. Forget Webb, forget that diary, forget everything you've seen here.
这是最后一次警告。下次见面，就不会是电话了。
This is your last warning. Next time we meet, it won't be over the phone.
晚安，Brennan先生。好好考虑。
Goodnight, Mr. Brennan. Think carefully.
……该死。
...Damn it.
这就是你的...律所？我以为我们在去垃圾填埋场的路上迷路了。
This is your... law firm? I thought we got lost on the way to the landfill.
我还是站着吧。Brennan先生，我们来这里是为了呼吸陈年灰尘，还是为了梳理案情？
I'll stand. Mr. Brennan, are we here to breathe vintage dust, or to sort out the case?
梳理。我们今天见了不少人，听了不少谎话。需要把它们拼起来。
Sort out. We saw a lot of people today, heard a lot of lies. Need to piece them together.
Rosa可以排除了。她只是个被利用的母亲。虽然她撒了谎，但动机是为了保护女儿。
Rosa is out. She's just a mother being used. She lied, but her motive was to protect her daughter.
同意。Morrison只是在恐吓她。倒是Tommy...那个记账的。
Agreed. Morrison was just threatening her. But Tommy... the bookkeeper.
他肯定知情。账目造假，还试图掩盖古董交易。Webb的勒索网络离不开他。
He definitely knows. Cooked books, tried to cover up the antique deals. Webb's extortion network couldn't run without him.
没错。但他胆子太小。他是个完美的共犯，但绝不是主谋。真正的危险信号在那两个人身上。
True. But he's too cowardly. He's the perfect accomplice, but definitely not the mastermind. The real red flags are on those two.
Jimmy和Vivian。
Jimmy and Vivian.
Jimmy平日伪装成隐形人。但他今天的崩溃...那种恐惧不是演出来的。他知道的比他说的多。
Jimmy pretends to be invisible. But his breakdown today... that fear wasn't acted. He knows more than he's saying.
他说话都在发抖...一直在说Anna，说孩子。我从没见过一个人这么绝望。
He was shaking when he spoke... kept talking about Anna, the kids. I've never seen someone so desperate.
那是他唯一在乎的事。O'Malley，人在绝望时会做任何事。但他对家人的担心不像是装的。
That's the only thing he cares about. O'Malley, people will do anything when desperate. But his worry for his family doesn't seem fake.
如果那个所谓的'要员'真的这么危险，为什么Jimmy还留在Webb身边？他明明知道Webb在玩火。
If this so-called 'Dignitary' is so dangerous, why did Jimmy stay with Webb? He knew Webb was playing with fire.
也许他离不开。也许他早就被卷进去了。有些人害怕真相被发现，有些人害怕自己的参与被发现。Jimmy是后者。
Maybe he can't leave. Maybe he's already dragged in. Some fear the truth being found, some fear their involvement being found. Jimmy is the latter.
但这不代表我也能放过Vivian。这里的传闻……这家歌舞厅的潜规则。她和Webb的关系绝对越过了'职业'的界限。如果她是他的情人，那她藏着的秘密可就太多了。

But that doesn't mean I can let Vivian off the hook. The rumors here... the unspoken rules of this cabaret. Her relationship with Webb definitely crossed the line of 'professional'. If she was his lover, she's hiding a lot more.
而且Jimmy最后把矛头指向了她。他说'问问Vivian'。一个被逼到绝境的人，为了保护家人，会扔出他手里最有价值的筹码。
And Jimmy pointed the finger at her in the end. He said 'ask Vivian'. A desperate man protecting his family will throw out his most valuable chip.
Morrison、Webb、Jimmy、还有那个被称为'Whale'的危险人物...Jimmy说Webb惹了不该惹的人。
Morrison, Webb, Jimmy, and that dangerous figure called 'Whale'... Jimmy said Webb messed with the wrong people.
你害怕了？
Scared?
不是害怕...是担心。Morrison虽然参与但警局在保护他，幕后黑手还逍遥法外...我不想你也出事。
Not scared... worried. Morrison is involved but protected by the force, the mastermind is still at large... I don't want you to get hurt.
我已经是被栽赃的凶手了。还能多糟？
I'm already the framed killer. How much worse can it get?
可以更糟。你可以真的死。
It can be worse. You could actually die.
那就在死之前查清真相。我们还剩多少时间？
Then let's find the truth before I die. How much time do we have left?
只有一天了。
Only one day left.
够了。走吧。我一直很小心，O'Malley。你也是。
Enough. Let's go. I'm always careful, O'Malley. You too.
那是当然。
Of course.
它能有效筛选客源。只有走投无路的人才会走进这里。
It filters clients effectively. Only those with nowhere else to go walk in here.
混乱有助于思考。整洁是留给那些无所事事的人的。
Chaos helps thinking. Tidiness is for those with nothing to do.
那我尽量争取留个全尸。最好还能穿得体面点。
Then I'll try to leave a whole corpse. Preferably dressed decently.
我们都会死，O'Malley。区别只在于故事是不是在我们手里结束。
We all die, O'Malley. The difference is whether the story ends in our hands.
Brennan先生……我能帮什么忙吗？我现在就在拖地，我...我还要赶着去清理大堂...您知道的，周末客人多，地板总是很脏...
Mr. Brennan... can I help you? I'm mopping... gotta clean the lobby... you know, busy weekend, dirty floors...
你知道Webb的勒索生意吗？
Do you know about Webb's extortion business?
什...什么？勒索？我...我不知道您在说什么！我只是个清洁工...我每天就是擦地拖地...我什么都不知道！真的！Brennan先生，我什么都不知道！
Wh... what? Extortion? I... I don't know what you're talking about! I'm just a cleaner... I just mop floors every day... I don't know anything! Really! Mr. Brennan, I don't know anything!
好，我明白了。继续干活吧。
Okay, I understand. Get back to work.
好...好的...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
O... okay... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
放轻松。我只想问问Jimmy最近有没有什么异常。你每天跟他擦肩，应该比我清楚。
Relax. Just asking about Jimmy. Any odd behavior? You see him daily, you'd know better than me.
Jimmy？他...他平常话少得要命，我也忙着赶场，很少...很少跟他说话...不过……等一下，让我想想——
Jimmy? He... implies barely speaks. I'm busy too, rarely... talk to him... But... wait, let me think -
慢慢来，记忆这种东西比地板顽固。
Take your time. Memory is stubborn, unlike dirt.
我记起来了！大概一周前...不，应该是五六天前...我在走廊擦墙...我正擦着呢，就看见Jimmy带了一个人进了Webb先生的会客室！
I remember! About a week ago... no, 5-6 days. I was cleaning the hallway wall... I saw Jimmy taking someone into Mr. Webb's parlor!
会客室，不是办公室？
Parlor, not office?
对对对！就是会客室！我当时还纳闷呢，那里没什么值钱的，重要的东西都在办公室嘛！
Yes yes! Parlor! I wondered why there, nothing valuable. Important stuff is in the office!
他进去多久？
How long was he inside?
不久，就两三分钟...他们就出来了...那个人穿得很板正，看着像是什么要员...但他出来的时候面色不太好看...阴沉沉的...
Not long, 2-3 minutes... they came out... that person was dressed very formally, looked like some dignitary... but he looked unwell when he came out... gloomy...
看见你了吗？
Did he see you?
看到了...他愣了一下，对我挤出个笑——那个笑好僵...我还以为他要说什么，但最后就...就匆忙走了...走得很快...
Saw me... froze, forced a smile - stiff smile... thought he'd speak, but just... rushed off... very fast...
之后呢？还出现过这种情况？
After that? Did it happen again?
没有……我就那一次看到...Brennan先生，我只是看见，人是他自己进去的...我真的...我只是在擦墙...我什么都没做...
No... only that one time... Mr. Brennan, I just saw him go in... I really... I was just cleaning the wall... I did nothing...
除了Jimmy，Vivian最近怎么样？你有没有注意到什么？
Besides Jimmy, how is Vivian lately? Noticed anything?
Vivian小姐...她最近状态很不好。我好几次看见她在角落里偷偷抹眼泪...而且...
Miss Vivian... she's been in a bad way. I've seen her crying in corners several times... and...
而且什么？特别是案发那晚。
And what? Especially on the night of the incident.
就是那晚！大概11点左右...我正准备去清理会客室门口的走廊...我看见Vivian小姐神情紧张地快步走过去。
That night! Around 11... I was about to clean the hall near the parlor... I saw Miss Vivian walking fast, looking very nervous.
又是会客室？她手里拿东西了吗？
The parlor again? Was she holding anything?
拿了！手里拿着个小包...看着不大，但她抓得很紧，像是怕摔了似的。她一看到我，马上把包往怀里藏，头也不回就进去了。
She was! Holding a small bag... didn't look big, but she gripped it tightly, like she was afraid to drop it. When she saw me, she hid the bag in her arms and rushed inside without looking back.
我知道了。你做得很好，Rosa。
I see. You did good, Rosa.
我、我不想惹麻烦...我只是个清洁工...我只希望我女儿能继续治病……医生说只要坚持用药...她就能慢慢好起来...但是药很贵...我必须保住这份工作...
I... I don't want trouble... just a cleaner... I just want my daughter to get treatment... Doctor said medicine helps... but it's expensive... I must keep this job...
Brennan先生，如果还有什么需要问的，我都会说实话，只求别让Morrison再吓我...他...他上次说要让我失业...如果我失业了，我女儿的药就买不起了...
Mr. Brennan, I'll tell the truth if you ask more, just don't let Morrison scare me... he... he threatened to fire me... if I lose my job, I can't afford my daughter's meds...
我会处理Morrison。你专心把地拖干净就行。你女儿的事，不用担心。
I'll handle Morrison. Just focus on mopping. Don't worry about your daughter.
谢谢您...谢谢您，Brennan先生...我...我一定好好工作...您...您也保重...那些人很危险...
Thank you... thank you Mr. Brennan... I... I'll work hard... You... take care... those people are dangerous...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 为了手术费，你会做傻事吗？

Words: 不会的！Brennan先生...医生说她需要手术...手术费还差很多...我每天都在发愁...但我绝不会因此做坏事...我只想靠自己的双手养活她...

No! Mr. Brennan... the doctor said she needs surgery... still short on surgery fees... I worry every day... but I would never do bad things because of it... I just want to support her with my own hands...
为了手术费，你会做傻事吗？
Would you do something foolish for the surgery fees?
Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You... you're here again? I... I told you everything before...
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details again.
好...好的...您请问吧...
O... okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。照顾好你女儿。
No more questions. Take care of your daughter.
谢谢您...Brennan先生...
Thank you... Mr. Brennan...
...这笔账不对...那个也不对...
...This entry is wrong... that one too...
Brennan...Brennan先生……又、又是您？您真的不睡觉的吗？
Mr. Brennan... y-you again? Do you really never sleep?
睡不着才来查案。Jimmy最近有没有哪天不像他自己？
Reviewing the case keeps me awake. Has Jimmy been acting unlike himself recently?
Jimmy？他……他一向话少，我也忙后台，很少……呃，很少跟他深聊...您知道的，各忙各的...
Jimmy? He... he's always quiet. I'm busy backstage, rarely... uh, rarely talk deep with him... You know, busy with our own things...
共事五年了，'很少深聊'不意味着你什么都不知道。
Five years working together. 'Rarely talk' doesn't mean you know nothing.
我们就是同事嘛！我在账房，他在厨房，井水不犯河水...我...我从来不多管闲事的...
Just colleagues! I'm accounts, he's kitchen. We don't cross paths... I... I never meddle...
那换个问题。他和Webb关系怎么样？
Different question then. How's his relationship with Webb?
挺好...挺好的。Webb先生信得过他，经常把他叫进办公室...有时候一谈就是半小时，门一关就两人，谁也别想靠近...
Good... pretty good. Mr. Webb trusts him, calls him into the office often... sometimes for half an hour. Door closed, just the two of them...
谈什么？
About what?
我真不知道！我不偷听的！这行的规矩就是别多嘴...我...我只是负责账目...
I really don't know! I don't eavesdrop! Rule of the trade, don't blab... I... I just handle the books...
规矩我懂。但你眼睛没闭上吧？
I know the rules. But your eyes weren't closed, were they?
我……我……前、前天算吗？我看到Jimmy从Webb办公室出来，怀里抱着个...个陶土水壶...
I... I... does the day before yesterday count? I saw Jimmy come out of Mr. Webb's office, holding a... a clay water jug...
什么样的水壶？
What kind of jug?
旧旧的，看着像古董...土黄色的，还有些裂纹...他用两只手捧着，走得很慢，眼睛盯着壶——像...像抱着他女儿一样...
Old, looked antique... earthy yellow, some cracks... He held it with both hands, walking slowly, eyes fixed on it—like... like he was holding his daughter...
然后？
Then?
他往厨房去。我正想问，他一看到我，就把壶藏到身后，挤出一个……很生硬的笑，然后就...就溜了...我...我从没见他那么慌张...
He headed to the kitchen. I was about to ask, but he saw me, hid the jug behind his back, forced a... very stiff smile, and then... slipped away... I... never saw him that panicked...
我...我明白...Brennan先生...
I... I understand... Mr. Brennan...
聊聊Vivian。大家都说她是Webb赚钱的工具。你怎么看？
Let's talk about Vivian. Everyone says she's just a tool for Webb to make money. What do you think?
我...我只是负责排她的班。Webb先生的生意...我不方便评论。
I... I just schedule her shifts. Mr. Webb's business... I can't comment on that.
别打官腔，Tommy。你天天看着她。她在这里开心吗？
Don't give me the official line, Tommy. You see her every day. Is she happy here?
开心？在这儿谁谈得上开心呢，Brennan先生。但是Vivian小姐...
Happy? Who can talk about happiness here, Mr. Brennan. But Miss Vivian...
作为一个有妻儿的男人...有些时候，真的很难看下去。
As a man with a wife and children... sometimes, it's really hard to watch.
很难看下去？
Hard to watch?
她本该是个好姑娘。如果这个世道能给像她这样的女孩子一份正当的工作，她就不必沦落到...被迫做那些事...
She should have been a good girl. If this world could give girls like her a proper job, she wouldn't have fallen to... being forced to do those things...
但我...我瞎说的！我只是个会计，我不该评论老板的...私事。请您忘了吧。
But I... I'm talking nonsense! I'm just an accountant, I shouldn't comment on the boss's... private matters. Please forget it.
放心。在这只有我们两个人。一点同情心不犯法。
Don't worry. Just us here. A little sympathy isn't a crime.
而且最近...Vivian小姐好像绷不住了。她取消了好几场VIP包厢的会客，说是身体不舒服...但我看她...是心里撑不下去了...
And lately... Miss Vivian seems to be breaking down. She canceled several VIP private room appointments, saying she was unwell... but I think... she just can't take it anymore...
好...好的...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
O... okay... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
没什么要问了。小心点，Tommy。
No more questions. Be careful, Tommy.
好...好的...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
O... okay... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You... you're here again? I... I told you everything before...
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details again.
好...好的...您请问吧...
O... okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。小心点，Tommy。
No more questions. Be careful, Tommy.
谢...谢谢您，Brennan先生...
Th... thank you, Mr. Brennan...
又是你。我是不是得把化妆室租给你？按小时收费，还是包月？
You again. Should I rent out my dressing room to you? Hourly rate, or monthly subscription?
我也想少来，但有些问题只有你能回答。
I'd like to come less often too, but you're the only one who can answer some questions.
也许能。也许没兴趣。
Maybe I can. Maybe I'm not interested.
Jimmy那天有些什么反常的举动吗？
Did Jimmy act unusually that day?
反常？如果你指那个平日像隐形人一样的家伙突然大吼大叫的话——是的，我听到了争吵。
Unusual? If you mean the invisible man suddenly screaming his head off—yes, I heard an argument.
隔着那种厚实包厢门都能听见？你确定没听错？
Through those thick parlor doors? Are you sure you heard right?
怎么确定是他？我看你也没那个闲心去辨认谁的声音吧。
How can you be sure? I doubt you care enough to identify voices.
确实。隔着门，声音像闷在罐子里，我也没那个本事分辨谁的声线。
True. Through the door, voices sound muffled like in a jar. I don't have the skill to distinguish voice lines either.
但我不需要耳朵。门开了，我刚好看到他们走出来。Jimmy脸色像吞了刀片一样...而且他不是一个人。
But I don't need ears. The door opened, and I happened to see them walk out. Jimmy looked like he swallowed razor blades... and he wasn't alone.
那个人脸色阴沉得也很难看。俩人一前一后，逃命似地走了。
That man looked gloomy too. One after another, they left like they were fleeing for their lives.
知道了。
Got it.
Tommy那天有什么反常吗？或者说他这个人，有没有什么奇怪的地方？
Was Tommy acting strange that day? Or is there anything odd about him in general?
那只耗子？有家室的正经老头一个。看我一眼都要脸红半天。
That mouse? A decent old man with a family. Blushes for half a day just looking at me.
就这些？
That's all?
他问过我，要不要离开这里。
He asked me once if I wanted to leave this place.
离开这里？去哪？
Leave here? To go where?
谁知道。也许是想当个英雄，救我脱离苦海？
Who knows. Maybe he wanted to play hero, save me from my misery?
不过...是个听话的人。让他帮点小忙，他从不拒绝。
But... he's obedient. Ask him a small favor, he never refuses.
什么样的小忙？
What kind of favors?
没什么。送个东西，传个话。你不会以为我让他杀人吧？
Nothing much. Deliver something, pass a message. You don't think I asked him to kill someone, do you?
没什么要问了。保重。
No more questions. Take care.
别再来了。这化妆室不是你的办公室。
Don't come back. This dressing room isn't your office.
你还想听什么？我没什么好说的了。
What else do you want to hear? I have nothing more to say.
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details.
快点问吧，我时间宝贵。
Ask quickly, my time is precious.
没有其他问题了。打扰了。
No more questions. Sorry to bother you.
下次记得先敲门。
Remember to knock next time.
你好，我是Zack Brennan，在调查Webb先生的案子。你是这里的厨师？
Hello, I'm Zack Brennan, investigating the Webb case. Are you the cook here?
我...我是Jimmy...主厨。有什么事吗？Webb先生...我听说了...
I... I'm Jimmy... head chef. Something wrong? Mr. Webb... I heard...

Parameters.ParameterStr: Webb的勒索生意，你参与过吗？

Words: 我昨天直到很晚都没看见你。你昨天去哪了？
I didn't see you around yesterday until late. Where were you?
在家里...先生。我妻子Anna...快生了...身体不好...我在照顾她。
Home... sir. My wife Anna... baby coming... not feeling well... I take care of her.
只来了大夜班...可以问Tommy...他帮我调班。大概...晚上9点？
Only night shift... you can ask Tommy... he change my schedule. Around... 9pm?
她现在怎么样？
How is she now?
还好...医生说...要小心...不能累着。
Ok now... doctor say... be careful... no tired.
那你前天晚上什么时候到这儿的？
What time did you get here the night before last?
好，照顾好你妻子。
Alright, take care of your wife.
谢谢...先生。
Thank you... sir.
糖...您吃很多糖...先生。压力大...是吗？
Candy... you eat lot candy... sir. Stress... yes?
......你观察力不错。继续干活吧。
......You're observant. Get back to work.
昨晚11点半左右，Webb出事的时候。你具体在哪？
Last night around 11:30, when Webb died. Where exactly were you?
11点10分开始...一直在厨房。快12点吧...都在那儿...帮厨Tom，洗碗工...做收尾清洁。
From 11:10... always in kitchen. Almost 12... all there... helper Tom, dishwashers... cleaning up.
打烊前...最忙。油污、垃圾、盘点...我没离开过。主厨不见...会有人注意到。
Before closing... very busy. Grease, trash, inventory... I not leave. Head chef gone... people would notice.
中间有没有出去过？哪怕一分钟？
Did you step out at all? Even for a minute?
没有...先生...一直在。Tom可以作证...洗碗工也在。
No... sir... always there. Tom can tell you... dishwashers too.
你听到什么动静没有？枪声，或者别的？
Did you hear anything? Gunshot, or anything else?
没有...厨房...很吵。后来...警笛响了...才知道出事。
No... kitchen... very loud. Later... sirens... then I know something happen.
好，我会找Tom核实。
Alright, I'll check with Tom.
好...先生。
Ok... sir.
Webb树敌不少。他还在搞一些肮脏的勒索生意。你有没有参与？或者有没有想过参与？
Webb had enemies. He ran some dirty extortion schemes. You ever get involved in that? Or think about it?
Webb先生？不...不...好老板。薪水高...也尊重人。
Mr. Webb? No... no... good boss. Good pay... respectful.
看...上周才拿了...'月度最佳员工'。我努力工作...先生。守规矩。'优秀员工'...不会卷进犯罪。我只是...煮饭的。
Look... just last week... 'Employee of the Month'. I work hard... sir. Follow rules. 'Excellent Employee'... not get in crimes. I just... cook.
即便你是非法移民？
Even though you're an illegal immigrant?
是...Webb先生知道...但没刁难过。去年生日...还送了英语书。
Yes... Mr. Webb know... but never give hard time. Birthday last year... give me English book.
行吧，月度最佳员工。希望你说的都是实话。
Alright, Employee of the Month. I hope you're telling the truth.
我...我不说谎...先生。我只是...煮饭的。
I... I not lie... sir. I just... cook.
没什么要问了。继续忙吧。
No more questions. Get back to work.
好...先生。
Ok... sir.
Brennan先生...您又来了？我之前都告诉您了...
Mr. Brennan... You're here again? I told you everything before...
有些细节我想再确认一下。
I want to confirm some details again.
好的...您请问吧...
Okay... please ask...
没有其他问题了。继续忙吧。
No more questions. Get back to work.
好的，Brennan先生。
Yes, Mr. Brennan.
谁？
Who?
Zack Brennan。还有一位...Emma O'Malley小姐。我们想问问Jimmy的事。
Zack Brennan. And... Miss Emma O'Malley. We want to ask about Jimmy.
Jimmy不在家。我们不想不想惹麻烦。请离开！
Jimmy isn't home. We don't want any trouble. Please leave!
安娜夫人，我们不是来找麻烦的。Webb先生出事了，我们只是担心Jimmy也被卷进去。
Mrs. Anna, we're not here to cause trouble. Mr. Webb is in trouble, and we're just worried Jimmy might be involved.
Jimmy...他只是个厨师。他什么都不知道。
Jimmy... he's just a cook. He knows nothing.
只有...一本书。Webb先生送的生日礼物。Jimmy最近老是半夜看它...但我没觉得有什么问题。
Only... a book. A birthday gift from Mr. Webb. Jimmy reads it late at night recently... but I don't see any problem with it.
能让我们看看那本书吗？
Can we see that book?
不行！那是私人物品。你们快走吧，我不想...
No! That's personal property. Please leave, I don't want to...
Ugh...! It hurts...
怎么了？
What's wrong?
抱歉...我的胃...老毛病了...可能是刚才走太急...
Sorry... my stomach... old problem... maybe walked too fast just now...
这里离医院还有段路...安娜夫人，能不能借杯热水？让她坐一会儿缓缓？
It's a way to the hospital... Mrs. Anna, could we borrow a cup of hot water? Let her sit and recover for a moment?
天哪...快进来。我有热水。你是孕妇吗？...哦不对，你太瘦了。
Oh my... come in quickly. I have hot water. Are you pregnant? ...Oh no, you're too thin.
谢谢...这种时候打扰您真是不好意思...
Thank you... so sorry to disturb you at a time like this...
没关系。我去烧点水，顺便拿个毯子。你脸色太白了。
It's okay. I'll boil some water, and get a blanket. You look so pale.
真是个好人...你动作快点。
Such a nice person... make it quick.
少废话。去卧室。我会拖住她。
Cut the crap. Go to the bedroom. I'll stall her.
别演过头了。
Don't overact.
也许他知道的比你以为的要多。比如...他最近有没有带什么奇怪的东西回来？或者藏起什么东西？
Maybe he knows more than you think. Like... did he bring anything strange home recently? Or hide anything?
Webb先生对他很器重吧？除了薪水，最近有没有额外给过他什么？或者...送过什么'礼物'？
Mr. Webb valued him highly, right? Besides salary, did he give him anything extra recently? Or... sent any 'gifts'?
你的演技越来越好了，O'Malley小姐。甚至骗过了我的良心——虽然只有一秒。
Your acting is getting better, Miss O'Malley. Even fooled my conscience—for a second.
干得好。尽量拖住她，别让她靠近卧室。我只需要两分钟。
Good job. Keep her occupied, don't let her near the bedroom. I just need two minutes.
0807...1920年8月7日，Vivian第一次在蓝月亮唱歌的日子。
0807... August 7th, 1920. The day Vivian first sang at the Blue Moon.
他人生中唯一真实的时刻...还没有堕落的时候。
The only genuine moment in his life... before the fall.
里面有什么？
What's inside?
什么都没有。
Nothing.
什么？
What?
被清空了。只剩灰烬...还有烧焦的纸片边缘。
Emptied. Only ashes left... and charred paper edges.
有人把证据都销毁了...
Someone destroyed all the evidence...
而且是最近。灰烬还有烟熏味，应该是案发后几天内。
And recently. The ashes still smell of smoke. Must have been within days of the murder.
Whale？
Whale?
能打开这个保险箱的人不多。要么知道密码，要么有钥匙...而且必须能自由进出Webb的办公室。
Not many people could open this safe. Either they knew the combination, or had a key... and they needed free access to Webb's office.
这说明Whale的人就在酒吧内部...甚至可能就是我们已经见过的人。
That means Whale's people are inside the bar... maybe even someone we've already met.
Whale比我们想象的更近。他不仅杀了Webb，还彻底清理了所有能指向他的证据。
Whale is closer than we thought. He didn't just kill Webb—he wiped out every trace that could lead to him.
那我们还有机会找到他吗？
Do we still have a chance to find him?
Webb如果真的准备了后手，不会只放在一个地方。录音设备的收据...他一定留下了什么。
If Webb really prepared a backup plan, he wouldn't put all his eggs in one basket. The recording equipment receipt... he must have left something.
保险箱被清空了...那谁杀了Webb？
The safe was emptied... then who killed Webb?
会不会...就是Whale亲自动的手？
Could it be... that Whale did it himself?
不。就算Webb的死和他有关，案发当天他也没有出现在酒吧。凶手一定是酒吧里的人——我们见过的某个人。找到真相，也许就能顺藤摸瓜，引出这个神秘人。

No. Even if Webb's death is connected to him, he wasn't at the bar that day. The killer has to be someone from the bar—someone we've already met. Find the truth, and maybe we can follow the trail to this mysterious figure.
回去现场再找找。一定还有什么我们没有发现的东西。
Let's go back to the scene and look again. There must be something we missed.
...这笔账不对...那个也不对...
...This entry is wrong... that one too...
Brennan...Brennan先生……又、又是您？您真的不睡觉的吗？
Mr. Brennan... y-you again? Do you really never sleep?
Jimmy最近有什么不对劲？
Has Jimmy been acting strange lately?
Jimmy？他……他话少，闷头干活。我...我不怎么关注厨房的事。
Jimmy? He... he's quiet, just works. I... I don't pay much attention to the kitchen.

Parameters.ParameterStr: 别装傻。Webb的脏钱，Jimmy没少经手吧？
你负责账目，总看得到些什么。

Words: 别装傻，Tommy。Jimmy是Webb最信任的人。Webb那些见不得光的钱，Jimmy没少经手吧？
Don't play dumb, Tommy. Jimmy was Webb's most trusted man. Jimmy handled Webb's dirty money, didn't he?
我...我看过一些单据。有时候Webb先生会让Jimmy去送'特殊的信'，回来后账上就会多一笔现金...
I... I've seen some receipts. Sometimes Mr. Webb made Jimmy deliver 'special letters', and cash would appear in the books after...
你负责账目，总看得到些什么。帮帮忙，Tommy。
You handle the books, you must see something. Help me out, Tommy.
...好吧。有时候Webb先生会让Jimmy去送一些'特殊的信'。回来后账上就多一笔现金...我...我只是记账的...
...Alright. Sometimes Mr. Webb made Jimmy deliver 'special letters'. Cash would appear after... I... I just keep the books...
Jimmy...他以前是个实诚人。但最近...最近他看着那些信封的眼神，像是在看炸弹。
Jimmy... he used to be honest. But lately... the way he looks at those envelopes, like he's looking at a bomb.
你知道他在帮Webb勒索？
You knew he was helping Webb blackmail people?
我能怎么办？我还有老婆孩子！Jimmy也一样...他女儿生病需要钱。Webb先生给的'奖金'很丰厚。我...我只能装作看不懂那些账目。
What could I do? I have a wife and kids! Jimmy's the same... his daughter is sick, needs money. Mr. Webb's 'bonuses' were generous. I... I just pretended not to understand the books.
前天那个陶土壶是怎么回事？
What about that clay jug the day before yesterday?
那天...他很慌。那个壶...看着不像Webb先生收藏的古董。Jimmy抱着它，脸煞白...我喊他，他像见鬼一样跑了。
That day... he was panicked. The jug... didn't look like one of Mr. Webb's antiques. Jimmy held it, face pale... I called him, he ran like he saw a ghost.
不仅是慌张，Brennan先生，那是...那是恐惧。像是手里捧着要命的东西。
Not just panic, Mr. Brennan, that was... terror. Like he was holding something that would claim a life.
Webb和Vivian是什么关系？
What's the relationship between Webb and Vivian?
Vivian是...是我们的头牌歌女。我负责前台排班...私事我不插手的……
Vivian is... our headliner. I handle scheduling... I don't get involved in private matters...

Parameters.ParameterStr: 有人说Vivian有杀人动机。你不说，警察来问你也脱不了干系。
帮我了解Vivian。我想帮她洗清嫌疑。

Words: 你在这管了五年账。别告诉我你不知道老板的私生活。有人说Vivian有杀人动机。你不说，警察来问，你家人也脱不了干系。

You've managed books here for five years. Don't tell me you know nothing of the boss's private life. People say Vivian has a motive for murder. If you stay silent, when the police come, your family will be dragged into this too.
什么？！不...不可能！她...她不会的！
What?! No... impossible! She... she wouldn't!
……好吧。Webb先生把她当另一半。他亲自把她从南区带回来，给她学费、衣服。她十七岁就被父母卖进妓院，是Webb先生半路拦下来的。
...Alright. Mr. Webb treated her like a partner. He brought her from the South Side, paid for lessons, clothes. She was sold to a brothel at 17, Webb intercepted.
帮我了解一下Vivian。如果她是清白的，我想帮她洗清嫌疑。
Help me understand Vivian. If she's innocent, I want to help clear her name.
...您是想帮她？好...好吧。Webb先生把她当另一半。她十七岁被父母卖进妓院，是Webb先生救了她。他是她唯一的依靠。
...You want to help her? Okay... Mr. Webb treated her like a partner. She was sold to a brothel at 17, Mr. Webb saved her. He's all she has.
Webb最近有什么变化？
How has Webb changed recently?
…最近两个月，Webb先生跟一个叫Rita的女人走得很近。她常来VIP包厢，点的都是最贵的酒...
...Last two months, Mr. Webb got close to a woman named Rita. She came to the VIP room often, ordered the most expensive wine...
Vivian知道吗？
Did Vivian know?
有一次我看到Vivian站在走廊，看着Webb先生和Rita。她没闹，只是转头走了。脚步很慢...像被掏空一样。
Once I saw Vivian in the hallway, watching Mr. Webb and Rita. She didn't make a scene, just turned and walked away. Slowly... like she was hollowed out.
后来我路过化妆室，听到她在里面哭，说：'我为他做了这么多。'
Later I passed her dressing room, heard her crying, saying: 'I did so much for him.'
Rita是谁介绍来的？
Who introduced Rita?
是一个客人介绍来的，说是能拓展生意。但后来...我偶然听到Webb先生在电话里说：'我知道她是谁派来的。但没关系，我会配合演戏。'
A guest introduced her, said she could expand business. But later... I overheard Mr. Webb on the phone saying: 'I know who sent her. But it's fine, I'll play along.'
他知道Rita的真实身份？
He knew who Rita really was?
我也不确定。但Webb先生提到过...'只要能保护重要的人'...我猜他说的是Vivian？
I'm not sure. But Mr. Webb mentioned... 'as long as it protects the important person'... I guess he meant Vivian?
Webb故意和Rita走近，是为了保护Vivian...
Webb got close to Rita on purpose, to protect Vivian...
老板死了，这店谁接管？
Boss is dead, who takes over this place?
Brennan先生，这儿上上下下几十号人——服务生、乐手、厨房杂工……大家都要吃饭啊。Webb先生生前交代过，如果他出了意外，让我先维持住局面。
Mr. Brennan, dozens of people here—waiters, musicians, kitchen staff... everyone needs to eat. Mr. Webb left instructions, if anything happened to him, I was to keep things running.

Parameters.ParameterStr: 代管久了，会不会想变成接管？
我不是怀疑你。只是想了解这店以后归谁。

Words: 代管久了，会不会想变成接管？Webb一死，这里的流水可就没人查了。为了这个杀人，理由很充分。
Manage it long enough, maybe you want to own it? With Webb dead, no one checks the cash flow. Good enough reason for murder.
别！千万别开这种玩笑！我有几斤几两自己清楚，哪有那个胆量？
Don't! Don't joke like that! I know my place, I don't have the guts!
我不是怀疑你。只是想了解这店以后归谁。
I'm not suspecting you. Just want to know who inherits this place.
...谢...谢谢您信我，Brennan先生。
...Th-thank you for trusting me, Mr. Brennan.
Webb先生早就跟家里人立过遗嘱了。他在英国那边还有亲戚。等消息传过去，那边应该会派人来处理遗产。
Mr. Webb already made a will with his family. He has relatives in England. Once news reaches them, they'll send someone to handle the estate.
这店不可能归我。我只想领薪水，绝不敢动歪心思！
The shop can't be mine. I just want my salary, I wouldn't dare have any wrong ideas!
今天就到这。保护好自己。别让别人知道你跟我说过什么。
That's all for today. Watch your back. Don't let anyone know what you told me.
我...我明白...谢谢您...
I... I understand... Thank you...
Brennan...Brennan先生...您...您又来了？我...我之前都说了...
Mr. Brennan... y-you're here again? I... I told you everything before...
有些事我还想再确认一下。
There are some things I want to confirm again.
好...好的...您...您请问吧...
O-okay... p-please ask...
没有其他问题了。继续忙吧。
No more questions. Get back to work.
好...好的，Brennan先生...
O-okay, Mr. Brennan...
这么早？看来这一行的勤奋程度比我想象的要高。
So early? Seems diligence in this industry is higher than I imagined.
我在排练今晚的新歌。Brennan先生，你知道吗？不管我在台下是什么身份……只要站在这里，我就只是个歌手。这是我这辈子唯一的梦想。
Rehearsing tonight's new song. Mr. Brennan, you know? No matter who I am offstage... standing here, I'm just a singer. It's the only dream I've ever had.
你觉得谁是凶手？
Who do you think is the killer?
谁知道呢？也许是仇家，也许是……某个被他伤透了心的倒霉鬼。
Who knows? Maybe an enemy, maybe... some poor soul he broke.
反正人都死了，我也没那个闲心去猜。比起抓凶手，我更在乎今晚的灯光有没有调好。
He's dead anyway, I don't care to guess. I'm more concerned about tonight's lighting.
昨晚派对是在这办的？
Was the party held here last night?
是的。就在这儿。灯光、香槟、还有那些虚伪的笑声……
Yes. Right here. Lights, champagne, and all that fake laughter...
不过Webb出事那会儿，客人们都已经走光了。只剩下我们这些……离不开这里的人。
But when Webb died, the guests were gone. Only us... those who can't leave.
听起来像是一场落寞的谢幕。
Sounds like a lonely curtain call.

Parameters.ParameterStr: 派对上发生了什么值得注意的事？

Words: 派对上发生了什么值得注意的事？
Did anything noteworthy happen at the party?
值得注意？……我只记得Webb全程都没看我一眼。
Noteworthy?... I only remember Webb didn't look at me once the whole time.
那晚你唱了什么歌？
What did you sing that night?
……你是第一个问我这个的人。
...You're the first person to ask me that.
其实……昨晚我特地为他安排了一首歌。那是压轴曲目，节奏很快，鼓点很重……足以把整个场子都炸掉。
Actually... I arranged a special song for him last night. The finale. Fast rhythm, heavy drums... enough to blow the roof off.
那是我非常喜欢的一首歌，我想把它送给他。
A song I really loved, meant for him.
足以炸掉场子的歌。
Enough to blow the roof off.
是啊。只可惜……Webb再也没机会听到了。或者说，就算他听到了，他也从来不在乎。
Yeah. Pity... Webb never got to hear it. Or rather, even if he did, he never cared.
你好好排练吧。
Keep rehearsing.
不送，Brennan先生。
See you, Mr. Brennan.
又是你？Brennan先生，你还想听什么？我没什么好说的了。
You again? Mr. Brennan, what else do you want to hear? I've got nothing more to say.
有些事我还想再确认一下。
There are some things I want to confirm again.
......随便吧。问完快走，别耽误我排练。
......Whatever. Ask and leave, don't waste my rehearsal time.
没有其他问题了。继续排练吧。
No more questions. Keep rehearsing.
不送。
See yourself out.
你也知道Webb惹了不该惹的人。既然这么危险，为什么还留在他身边？
You know Webb messed with the wrong people. If it's so dangerous, why stay with him?
我需要这份工作，Brennan先生。Anna快生了...医药费、房租...我不能失业。
I need this job, Mr. Brennan. Short and simple. I'm short on cash, and I can't afford to be unemployed.
上次说到那个陶壶。你偷东西我能理解，缺钱嘛。
About that jug last time. I get the stealing, you needed money.
但有一件事我想不通——你在这干了多久？两年？三年？
But one thing I can't figure out—how long have you worked here? Two years? Three?
三年零四个月，先生。
Three years and four months, sir.

Parameters.ParameterStr: 三年都没动手。偏偏Webb死前几天你下手了。
三年忍下来不容易。是什么让你突然破例？

Words: 三年都没动手。偏偏Webb死前几天你下手了。
Three years and you never touched anything. Then just days before Webb dies, you make your move.
你提前知道他要死？
Did you know he was going to die?
三年忍下来不容易。是什么让你突然破例？时机太巧了。
Holding back for three years isn't easy. What made you suddenly break your rule? The timing is too convenient.
你知道他快死了？
Did you know he was about to die?
什...什么？我怎么可能知道！Brennan先生，我只是个厨子...
Wha... what? How could I possibly know! Mr. Brennan, I'm just a cook...
我就是...那天实在撑不住了，先生。Anna催着要钱...医院说要先交押金...
I just... couldn't hold on anymore that day, sir. Anna was pressing for money... the hospital said we need to pay a deposit first...
杀人？不...不不不，Brennan先生！Webb先生给我工作、给我活路，我怎么会害他？
Kill? No... no no no, Mr. Brennan! Mr. Webb gave me a job, gave me a way to live, why would I hurt him?
我承认我偷了东西...但我不是杀人犯，先生...我只是个厨子...
I admit I stole things... but I'm not a murderer, sir... I'm just a cook...
Webb为什么要和你合作？一个厨子，帮他打理勒索生意？
Why would Webb work with you? A cook, helping him run an extortion business?
我不知道，Brennan先生。Webb先生从来没解释过。
I don't know, Mr. Brennan. Mr. Webb never explained.
有时候让我帮他送东西给一些人，收收钱...打打下手。可能是看我手脚勤快，嘴巴又紧吧。
Sometimes he had me deliver things to people, collect money... do odd jobs. Maybe he liked that I work fast and keep my mouth shut.
你是个聪明人，Jimmy。观察力也不差。
You're a smart guy, Jimmy. Sharp eyes too.
...Brennan先生，我以前是个好学生。在老家的时候，我是班里第一名。但是...
...Mr. Brennan, I used to be a good student. Back home, I was first in my class. But...
家里不让念了。他们说书本不能当饭吃。在老家打工，一个月才几块钱...连糊口都难。这里虽然脏、累，但至少...能攒下点钱。

Family wouldn't let me continue. Said books don't put food on the table. Working back home, just a few dollars a month... barely enough to survive. Here it's dirty and hard, but at least... I can save a little.
我只想攒够钱，搞定身份...拿到公民权，先生。也许那样，我就不用再躲躲藏藏了。
I just want to save enough, get my status sorted... get citizenship, sir. Maybe then, I won't have to hide anymore.
我就一句话，Jimmy。公民权救不了死人。注意安全。
Just one thing, Jimmy. Citizenship won't save a dead man. Watch your back.
我知道。但现在...这炉子能付账单。
I know. But right now... this stove pays the bills.
又是您？Brennan先生，我该说的都说过了...
You again? Mr. Brennan, I've already said everything I know...
有些事我还想再确认一下。
There are some things I want to confirm again.
好吧...您问吧。但我真的不知道还能说什么了。
Alright... ask away. But I really don't know what else I can tell you.
没有其他问题了。继续忙吧。
No more questions. Get back to work.
好的，Brennan先生。
Yes, Mr. Brennan.
就是现在了，Emma。Morrison给的三天期限……只剩下这最后一个晚上了。
This is it, Emma. The three days Morrison gave us... we only have this last night left.
那我们就不能再犯错了。今晚必须找出真正的凶手。让我们最后再分析一次那些矛盾点。
Then we can't afford any more mistakes. We must find the real killer tonight. Let's go over the contradictions one last time.
案发现场，我们能进去吗？Webb的会客室应该还有很多东西没被发现。
The crime scene, can we get in? Webb's reception room should still have many things undiscovered.
刚才我看到警员已经撤走了。Morrison那边的人手不够，不可能24小时守着现场。
I just saw the officers leave. Morrison doesn't have enough men to guard the scene 24 hours.
那我们就趁这个空档进去。
Then we'll slip in during this window.
也许可以悄悄进去看看。不过要小心，别被人发现。现场可能还留着什么被忽略的线索。
Maybe we can sneak in. But be careful, don't get caught. The scene might still have overlooked clues.
外面的垃圾桶上有个弹孔。弹道很陡峭……是从高处射下来的。
The bullet hole in the trash can outside. The trajectory was steep... from high above.
它不是从Webb办公室窗户射出的。它是从更高的地方射出的。
It didn't come from Webb's office window. It came from somewhere higher.
11点半的时候，大家都以为那只是外面常见的帮派闹事。没人惊慌。
At 11:30, everyone thought it was just the usual gang trouble outside. No one panicked.
直到大家发现了尸体。然后那个"随机"的噪音就在大家的记忆里变成了"凶器"。那不是信号，侦探。那是个诱饵。
Until they found the body. Then that 'random' noise became the 'murder weapon' in everyone's memory. It wasn't a signal, Detective. It was a decoy.
如果他早就死了，我们应该能听到枪声。马格南的声音可不小。
If he died earlier, we should have heard a gunshot. A Magnum isn't quiet.
没错。在拥挤的酒吧里要想安静是不可能的。除非酒吧当时并不安静。
True. Silence is impossible in a crowded bar. Unless the bar wasn't silent.
乐队。晚上11点的《艾斯弗德狂想曲》。
The band. 'Ashford Rhapsody' at 11:00 PM.
有这种可能。鼓声可以掩盖枪声。但这需要完美的时间掌控。是凶手计划好的，还是只是运气好？我们得决定这是否是我们愿意相信的巧合。
It's a possibility. The drums could mask the shot. But that relies on perfect timing. Did the killer plan it, or did they just get lucky? We have to decide if that's a coincidence we believe in.
Vivian在11点05分敲的门。门锁着，但通过门传来了声音。
Vivian knocked at 11:05. The door was locked, but she heard a voice.
同样有两种解释。一：Webb还活着，为了隐私锁了门，然后叫她离开。
Two interpretations again. One: Webb was alive, locked the door for privacy, and told her to leave.
二：Webb已经死了。凶手锁了门。
Two: Webb was already dead. The killer locked the door.
那声音呢？Vivian说听起来很"生硬"。是一个疲惫的人？还是一个试图把目击者挡在门外的模仿者？那扇锁着的门保护着里面的人……不管是死人还是活人。
And the voice? Vivian said it sounded 'stiff'. Was it a tired man? Or a mimic trying to keep a witness out? The locked door protects the occupant... whoever that was.
Brennan先生...Miss O'Malley...你们怎么会来这里？
Mr. Brennan... Miss O'Malley... Why are you here?
Anna，我们特意过来...是想告诉你关于Jimmy的事。
Anna, we came specifically... to tell you about Jimmy.
Jimmy？他昨天还说今晚回来做饭...他是不是被警局扣留了？
Jimmy? He said yesterday he'd be back to cook tonight... Is he detained by the police?
不，Anna。我很抱歉...他没能挺过去。
No, Anna. I'm sorry... he didn't make it.
什么...？不...这不可能...
What...? No... That's impossible...
是真的。那些人...那个叫'Whale'的人，用你和孩子的命威胁他。Jimmy是为了保护你才...
It's true. Those people... the one called 'Whale', threatened him with your life and the baby's. Jimmy did it to protect you...
为了我...那个傻瓜...
For me... that fool...
那天晚上他很慌张...他说如果他回不来，就把地板下面这个铁盒交给信得过的人。
That night he was panicked... he said if he didn't come back, give this iron box under the floor to someone I trust.
Brennan先生，你是唯一一个在他临死前陪着他的人。请您收下它。如果是为了保护我们，这里面一定是很重要的东西。
Mr. Brennan, you were the only one with him before he died. Please take it. If it was to protect us, there must be something important inside.
我收到了，是个很沉的铁盒...这是？里面装满了破碎的黑色碎片...看起来像是被故意损毁的黑胶唱片。Anna，你知道这是什么吗？
I've got it, it's heavy... What's this? Filled with broken black fragments... looks like a deliberately destroyed vinyl record. Anna, do you know what this is?
我不知道...我从来没打开看过。但请您务必小心。Jimmy说...那个让他这么做的人，是魔鬼。
I don't know... I never opened it. But please be careful. Jimmy said... the person who made him do this is a devil.
如果这真是唱片，那这些碎片里就藏着Jimmy拼死想要留下的真相。Emma，我们回办公室。我要尝试修复它，听听里面到底记录了什么。
If this is really a record, then these fragments hide the truth Jimmy died to protect. Emma, back to the office. I need to try to fix it and hear what's recorded inside.
让我把这些碎片拼起来...希望能播放。
Let me piece these fragments together... hope it plays.

Speaker: _RECORDER
Words: “...Moore银行注资已到位...南区清退计划启动...”
...Moore Bank injection confirmed... South Side clearance initiated...

Speaker: _RECORDER
Words: “...我们没有时间了，名单上的‘钉子’必须在11月初彻底消失...”
...We're out of time, 'nails' on the list must disappear by early November...

Speaker: _RECORDER
Words: “...1912年家属名单确认...第一批目标：Margaret Brennan...鞋坊...”
...1912 Family List confirmed... First target: Margaret Brennan... Shoe Shop...
Margaret Brennan... 鞋坊...？不...这怎么可能...
Margaret Brennan... Shoe Shop...? No... how is this possible...
Brennan先生，那是你母亲的名字！还有‘1912年家属’...这是什么意思？
Mr. Brennan, that's your mother's name! And '1912 Family'... what does this mean?
Webb死了，Jimmy死了...这根本不是什么简单的商业清退，这是一场有预谋的清洗行动！
Webb died, Jimmy died... This isn't just commercial clearance, it's a premeditated purge!
录音里说‘11月初消失’...今天是11月5日，他们这就是这几天动手！
The recording said 'disappear by early November'... today is November 5th, they are acting these days!
他们已经在清理‘钉子’了。Jimmy是昨晚，下一个就是她！我得马上赶去鞋坊！
They are already clearing the 'nails'. Jimmy was last night, she is next! I must rush to the Shoe Shop!
坐我的车，我来开！快走！
Take mine, I'll drive! Let's go!
Zack，坐稳了！
Zack, hold tight!
再快点！Emma！求你了，再快点！母亲还在店里！
Faster! Emma! Please, faster! Mother is still in the shop!
我已经把油门踩到底了！前面就是Maple街区！
I've floored it! Maple Street is just ahead!
那是...前面那是...烟？！
That... that ahead... Smoke?!
Mom——!!
Vivian，还有个细节需要确认。
Vivian, there is one more detail I need to confirm.
Brennan先生，我该说的都说了。您还想知道什么？
Mr. Brennan, I've said everything I need to say. What else do you want to know?

Parameters.ParameterStr: 那天晚上你几点去的Webb会客室？

Words: 那天晚上你几点去的Webb会客室？
What time did you go to Webb's parlor that night?
就在那首歌的时候。大概11点05分。
During that song. Around 11:05.
但是至少那首歌结束，我都看到Webb的房间门是紧闭的。那会儿他应该还没出事。
But at least until that song ended, I saw Webb's door was tightly closed. He probably hadn't been killed yet.
之后我就一直待在化妆室里面了。
After that, I stayed in the dressing room the whole time.
你敲门了吗？见到他了吗？
Did you knock? Did you see him?
敲了几下，听到有人喊'别打扰我'。当时觉得是Webb，不过细想...那时候音乐那么大，确实没有听清。
I knocked a few times, heard someone shout 'Don't disturb me'. At the time I thought it was Webb, but thinking about it... the music was so loud, I really didn't hear clearly.
知道了。
Got it.
11点半那声枪响，你听到了吗？
Did you hear that gunshot at 11:30?
枪声？
Gunshot?
...听到了。但我以为是外面的帮派在闹事。这附近三天两头就有枪声，谁会当回事？
...I heard it. But I thought it was gang trouble outside. Gunshots happen every other day around here, who would care?
所以你没觉得异常？
So you didn't think it was unusual?
没有。那会儿我刚从Webb门口回来，心情不好，躲回化妆室补妆。外面打不打枪，跟我有什么关系。
No. I had just come back from Webb's door, in a bad mood, hiding in the dressing room fixing my makeup. Whether there's shooting outside has nothing to do with me.
也就是说——你没看到任何人往外面开枪？
So — you didn't see anyone shooting outside?
我又不是守夜人。那会儿我在镜子前面，不是窗户前面。
I'm not a night watchman. I was in front of the mirror, not the window.
知道了。
Got it.
没什么要问了。
No more questions.
慢走，Brennan先生。
Take care, Mr. Brennan.
Brennan先生，您这是第几次来了？我真的没什么可说的了。
Mr. Brennan, how many times is this now? I really have nothing left to say.
还有几个细节需要核实。
A few more details need verification.
......行吧。问完快走。
......Fine. Ask and leave.

Parameters.ParameterStr: 那天晚上你几点去的Webb会客室？

Words: 没有其他问题了。打扰了。
No more questions. Sorry to bother you.
希望这是最后一次。
I hope this is the last time.
这是循环1和Morrison的对话（占位）
This is Loop 1 dialogue with Morrison (placeholder)
Brennan……我很遗憾。死者应该就是Margaret。我是第一响应警官，我当然有初步的调查信息。（占位）
Brennan... I'm sorry. The victim should be Margaret. I'm the first responding officer, of course I have preliminary investigation information. (placeholder)
这是循环2和Leonard的对话（占位）
This is Loop 2 dialogue with Leonard (placeholder)
Vinnie Moretti？我不认识这个人。Frank Kowalski？是的，他是我们的客户。（占位）
Vinnie Moretti? I don't know this person. Frank Kowalski? Yes, he was our client. (placeholder)
Leonard否认案发当晚去过鞋坊。（占位）
Leonard denies going to the shoe shop on the night of the incident. (placeholder)
那天晚上我整晚在家，从没去过鞋坊。（占位）
I was home all night, never went to the shoe shop. (placeholder)
Frank Kowalski？我知道这个名字。但是我最近2、3个月没去过他那儿。（占位）
Frank Kowalski? I know the name. But I haven't been near his place for two or three months. (placeholder)
Frank亲自签字借了$3,200。我们的协议条款完全符合法律规定。（占位）
Frank personally signed for $3,200. Our agreement terms fully comply with legal requirements. (placeholder)
我叔叔Frank一直是个正直的人，直到最近那个墨西哥女人出现。（占位）
My uncle Frank was always an upright man, until that Mexican woman showed up recently. (placeholder)
我和Frank恋爱5年了，从1940年开始。（占位）
Frank and I have been in love for five years, since 1940. (placeholder)
我愿意按照Frank的意愿将房产捐给工人互助会。（占位）
I'm willing to donate the property to the Workers' Mutual Aid Society as Frank wished. (placeholder)
Rose提供案发当晚的目击证词。（占位）
Rose provides eyewitness testimony from the night of the incident. (placeholder)
那天晚上...我在窗户边等Frank。（占位）
That night... I was waiting for Frank by the window. (placeholder)
这是循环2和O'Hara的对话（占位）
This is Loop 2 dialogue with O'Hara (placeholder)
银行的人来催款，还派了一个意大利人来威胁我。（占位）
The bank people came to collect, and even sent an Italian man to threaten me. (placeholder)
这是循环2和Tony的对话（占位）
This is Loop 2 dialogue with Tony (placeholder)
这是循环3和Moore的对话（占位）
This is Loop 3 dialogue with Moore (placeholder)
这是循环4和Danny的对话（占位）
This is Loop 4 dialogue with Danny (placeholder)
这是循环4和Rose的对话（占位）

This is Loop 6 opening dialogue with Emma (placeholder)
Morrison，我有一些问题要问你。看这张照片——死者的手上没有戒指。
Morrison, I have some questions for you. Look at this photo - there's no ring on the victim's hand.
那又怎样？也许她不戴戒指。Brennan，你在浪费时间。
So what? Maybe she doesn't wear a ring. Brennan, you're wasting time.
我母亲从不摘下她的婚戒。从我父亲去世后的二十年里，她从未摘下过。而且首饰盒是空的——真金不怕火炼，Morrison。我在尸体周围仔细搜索过，没有发现任何金属戒指的残留。

My mother never took off her wedding ring. In the twenty years since my father's death, she never once removed it. And the jewelry box is empty - real gold survives fire, Morrison. I searched thoroughly around the body and found no metal ring residue.
......好吧，也许我的判断有些草率。但这不能证明死者不是Margaret。
...Fine, maybe my judgment was hasty. But this doesn't prove the victim isn't Margaret.
你说你有尸检调查信息。但我让Emma联系了Foster——她说尸检报告需要明天才能完成，现在连性别都无法确定。
You said you had autopsy information. But I had Emma contact Foster - she said the autopsy report won't be ready until tomorrow, they can't even determine gender yet.
我是第一响应警官，我当然有初步的调查信息...
I'm the first responding officer, of course I have preliminary investigation info...
你根本没有任何'尸检调查信息'。你只是基于环境猜测，然后用'专业权威'来唬我。Morrison，你为什么要误导我？
You don't have any 'autopsy information' at all. You just guessed based on the environment, then used 'professional authority' to intimidate me. Morrison, why are you misleading me?
好吧，Brennan。你说得对，我没有正式的尸检结果。
Fine, Brennan. You're right, I don't have official autopsy results.
我恨你，Brennan。你揭发我迷晕你、栽赃陷害的事，毁了我在警局的声誉。
I hate you, Brennan. You exposed me for drugging you and framing you, ruined my reputation at the precinct.
我不知道死者到底是谁。但……我收到过一个匿名电话，火灾当天早上。有人让我把死者认定为Margaret。
I don't know who the victim really is. But... I received an anonymous call, the morning of the fire. Someone told me to identify the victim as Margaret.
谁打的电话？
Who made the call?
我不知道。但他给了我一个条件——你给我一个能结案的答案，我就去查你母亲的下落。
I don't know. But he gave me a condition - you give me an answer that closes the case, and I'll look into your mother's whereabouts.
Leonard，你说你不认识Vinnie Moretti？
Leonard, you say you don't know Vinnie Moretti?
Vinnie Moretti？我不认识这个人。
Vinnie Moretti? I don't know this person.
Vinnie看到你的照片，脱口而出'Russo'。我去Moore银行调查了你的入职档案——原名Leonard Russo，1942年改名为Leonard Ross。很多移民改名融入社会，但Vinnie认识的是'Russo'。

Vinnie saw your photo and blurted out 'Russo'. I checked your employment records at Moore Bank - original name Leonard Russo, changed to Leonard Ross in 1942. Many immigrants change names to fit in, but Vinnie knows you as 'Russo'.
很多移民都会改名字融入社会！这不能证明什么。
Many immigrants change their names to fit in! This doesn't prove anything.
你说和Vinnie没有来往？那这些钞票怎么解释？O'Hara太太上交了贷款催款时缺失的连号钞票，而Vinnie三天前在Silver Moon酒吧花的钞票——编号完全对应。

You say you have no contact with Vinnie? Then how do you explain these bills? Mrs. O'Hara turned in sequential bills missing from the loan collection, and the bills Vinnie spent at Silver Moon bar three days ago - the serial numbers match perfectly.
我承认我认识Vinnie，我以前叫Russo，但那又怎样？那些钱是我借给Vinnie的。
I admit I know Vinnie, I used to be called Russo, but so what? That money was lent to Vinnie.
借给他的？Silver Moon酒吧老板Tony告诉我，Vinnie每次来都说'今天又赚了一笔'，出手阔绰。这不像在还债。再看这本工作日记——上面记录了日期、目标、地点、金额，每笔后面都打了勾。这是雇佣关系，不是借钱。

Lent to him? Silver Moon bar owner Tony told me Vinnie always says 'Made another buck today', spends generously. That's not how someone repays debt. And look at this work diary - dates, targets, locations, amounts, each entry checked off. This is employment, not borrowing.
好吧，Brennan。你拿着这些证据去法院告我是没有用的。我承认Vinnie帮我工作，但这和Frank的案子无关。
Fine, Brennan. Taking this evidence to court won't help you. I admit Vinnie works for me, but this has nothing to do with Frank's case.
好吧，Brennan。你证明了我和Vinnie的关系。但你知道吗？你拿这些去法院，法官会告诉你——'这不构成犯罪'。
Fine, Brennan. You've proven my relationship with Vinnie. But you know what? Take this to court and the judge will tell you - 'This doesn't constitute a crime'.
也许。但我现在知道了足够多。接下来我会去找你的老板——Moore。
Maybe. But now I know enough. Next I'll be visiting your boss - Moore.
Moore先生，你说这份贷款是Frank亲自签字的？请看这个——Frank在牛奶订购单上的真实签名，颤抖歪斜、墨迹深浅不一。再看银行档案上的签名——工整流畅、一气呵成。这不是同一个人签的。

Mr. Moore, you say Frank signed this loan himself? Look at this - Frank's real signature on a milk order receipt, trembling and unsteady. Now look at the signature on the bank file - neat and flowing. These weren't signed by the same person.
Frank年纪大了，Leonard只是好心帮忙。Frank本人口头确认过所有内容。
Frank is old, Leonard was just helping. Frank verbally confirmed everything.
合法？那我们一条一条来看。第5条：'月利率12%，按月复利计算。'年化是289%。伊利诺伊州法律规定消费者贷款年化利率上限是7%。289%对7%，超标40倍。

Legal? Let's go through it one by one. Article 5: 'Monthly interest rate 12%, compounded monthly.' That's 289% annually. Illinois law caps consumer loan interest at 7% annually. 289% versus 7%, that's 40 times over the limit.
再看第7条：利滚利。州法明文禁止。还有每月$50管理费——一年$600，等于本金的100%。这就是为什么Frank每月还钱，债务却越还越多！

And Article 7: compound interest on interest. Explicitly prohibited by state law. Plus $50 monthly management fee - $600 a year, 100% of the principal. This is why Frank's debt grew larger the more he paid!
Brennan，你去告我。你拿着这些证据去法院，法官会告诉你'Moore先生没有违法'。
Brennan, go ahead and sue me. Take this evidence to court, the judge will tell you 'Mr. Moore has not broken any law'.
你说得对。这份协议的抬头——不是'Moore Bank Loan Agreement'，而是'Moore Investment Company Investment Agreement'。投资公司不受银行法7%利率上限限制。但Frank的日记里写的是'去Moore银行借钱'。他根本不知道自己签的是投资协议。

You're right. This agreement header - it's not 'Moore Bank Loan Agreement', it's 'Moore Investment Company Investment Agreement'. Investment companies aren't bound by the bank's 7% rate cap. But Frank wrote in his diary 'going to Moore Bank to borrow money'. He had no idea he was signing an investment agreement.
是的，这是投资公司的投资协议...但这是合法的商业操作。
Yes, this is an investment company's investment agreement... but this is a legal business operation.
我只是借钱给他...我没想杀他。Brennan，我可以帮你。如果能先找到真正的杀人犯...我可以提供一些东西。
I just lent him money... I didn't want to kill him. Brennan, I can help you. If you find the real killer first... I can provide something.
什么东西？
What is it?
Frank的遗嘱。他在我们银行保管了一份副本。你可能会对里面的内容感兴趣——特别是关于一个叫Rose Martinez的女人。
Frank's will. He kept a copy at our bank. You might be interested in what's in it - especially regarding a woman named Rose Martinez.
Danny，你说Rose'最近'才勾引你叔叔。但Margaret鞋坊地下室里，这个旧沙发扶手磨损严重，坐垫凹陷至少3-5年。窗帘褪色严重，地板有长期磨痕。这些都不是'最近'的痕迹。

Danny, you say Rose 'recently' seduced your uncle. But in Margaret's shoe shop basement, this old sofa has severely worn armrests, cushions sunken for at least 3-5 years. The curtains are badly faded, the floor has long-term wear marks. None of this is 'recent'.
就算他们认识久了...她还是骗子！我叔叔欠银行$3,200，都是因为她！
Even if they've known each other a long time... she's still a con artist! My uncle owes the bank $3,200, all because of her!
骗他贷款？看看贷款申请表——用途栏写的是'房屋维修——Margaret的地下室'。这些维修收据也证实了。Frank贷款是为了修这个地下室，不是因为Rose。而Rose自己还曾劝他别贷款。

Tricked him into a loan? Look at the loan application - the purpose says 'House Repair - Margaret's Basement'. These repair receipts confirm it. Frank took the loan to fix this basement, not because of Rose. And Rose herself tried to talk him out of borrowing.
遗嘱写得明明白白！Rose Martinez，继承房产！她就是为了钱！
The will says it clearly! Rose Martinez, inherits the property! She's in it for the money!
遗嘱？你只看了主文。看看这个手写附加条款：'若Rose不愿接受，全部捐给工人互助会。'Frank给了Rose选择权。
The will? You only read the main text. Look at this handwritten addendum: 'If Rose doesn't wish to accept, donate everything to the Workers' Mutual Aid Society.' Frank gave Rose the choice.
口头不作数！她嘴上说不要，心里想着全拿走！
Words don't count! She says no with her mouth but wants to take everything!
我愿意签字。我现在就签字放弃所有继承权。我不要他的房子。我只想要他活着。
I'm willing to sign. I'll sign right now to renounce all inheritance rights. I don't want his house. I just want him alive.
......我只是想...叔叔的房子在拆迁区...那笔补偿金...我真的很缺钱...
...I just wanted... uncle's house is in the demolition zone... that compensation money... I really need money...
所以你诬陷Rose，是为了拿到Frank的房产继承权。拆迁补偿比房子本身值钱得多。
So you slandered Rose to get Frank's property inheritance. The demolition compensation is worth far more than the house itself.
Vinnie，你说最近没去过Frank那儿。那你能解释一下，为什么在Margaret鞋坊废墟里发现了你的打火机吗？这打火机表面有你名字首字母'V'的划痕。

Vinnie, you say you haven't been near Frank's place recently. Then can you explain why your lighter was found in the ruins of Margaret's shoe shop? This lighter has your initial 'V' scratched on the surface.
而且，有目击者看到你11月6日晚上9点多从鞋坊出来，手里拿着打火机。
And a witness saw you leaving the shoe shop after 9 PM on November 6th, holding a lighter.
好！够了！我承认！是我！是我放的火！Frank是我杀的！
Enough! Fine! I confess! It was me! I set the fire! I killed Frank!
11月6日晚上，我去了Frank家。我们发生了争执。我...我一时失控，拿起烛台砸了他！然后放火想毁灭证据！
On the night of November 6th, I went to Frank's place. We had an argument. I... I lost control, grabbed the candlestick and hit him! Then set fire to destroy evidence!
Zack...有问题。他认罪认得太快了。
Zack... something's wrong. He confessed too quickly.
Brennan！我知道你母亲在哪里！
Brennan! I know where your mother is!
Mickey！你这个叛徒！你背叛了约定！你出卖了Leo！
Mickey! You traitor! You broke the deal! You sold out Leo!
Leonard，你说那天晚上你整晚在家。但是鞋坊废墟中发现的烛台上，嵌着一块眼镜碎片。而我母亲Margaret证实——她8点20分看到一个戴眼镜的西装男匆匆离开鞋坊。

Leonard, you say you were home all night. But a candlestick found in the shoe shop ruins has an eyeglass fragment embedded in it. And my mother Margaret confirmed - she saw a bespectacled man in a suit hurrying away from the shoe shop at 8:20 PM.
就算现场有眼镜碎片，也不能证明是我的。很多人都戴眼镜。
Even if there are eyeglass fragments at the scene, it doesn't prove they're mine. Many people wear glasses.
我注意到一个有趣的细节。你的眼镜——两只镜片颜色不一样。左眼镜片是透明的，看起来很新。但右眼镜片偏黄，明显是旧的。你最近换过左镜片，对吧？

I noticed an interesting detail. Your glasses - the two lenses are different colors. The left lens is clear and looks new. But the right lens is yellowish, clearly old. You recently replaced the left lens, didn't you?
也许我该去附近的配镜店查查，有没有人最近换过左镜片。
Maybe I should check the nearby optician's, see if someone recently replaced a left lens.
好吧...我承认...我去过鞋坊。我只是去拜访Frank，想劝他不要和银行作对。我们争吵了，我的眼镜不小心碎了。但我没有伤害他！
Fine... I admit it... I went to the shoe shop. I was just visiting Frank, trying to talk him out of fighting the bank. We argued, my glasses accidentally broke. But I didn't hurt him!
只是拜访？我母亲听到了激烈的争吵声，摔东西的声音。那个烛台上不仅有眼镜碎片——还有凹痕，和Frank头部的伤口吻合。
Just a visit? My mother heard a violent argument, the sound of things being smashed. That candlestick doesn't just have eyeglass fragments - it has a dent, matching the wound on Frank's head.
Leonard，Vinnie已经认罪了。他替你扛下了一切。你知道他为什么这么做吗？
Leonard, Vinnie has already confessed. He took the fall for everything. Do you know why he did it?
是！我承认！我用烛台打了他！我和Frank在鞋坊里发生了争执！我很生气，我抓起烛台打了他的头！他倒在地上，头上流血...但是侦探，我离开的时候他还活着！只是受伤！

Yes! I admit it! I hit him with the candlestick! Frank and I argued in the shoe shop! I was angry, I grabbed the candlestick and hit his head! He fell to the ground, bleeding... But detective, he was still alive when I left! Just injured!
你说你离开时Frank'受伤倒在地上'。但Vinnie的认罪证词里说——他看到Frank'正在床上睡觉'。不是受伤倒在地上，而是在床上睡觉。
You say when you left Frank was 'injured on the ground'. But in Vinnie's confession, he says he saw Frank 'sleeping in bed'. Not injured on the ground, but sleeping in bed.
答案很简单：Vinnie到场时，Frank已经死了。是你杀了他。Vinnie发现尸体后，把Frank搬到床上，布置成'睡着'的样子，然后放火。他以为这样就能保护你。

The answer is simple: when Vinnie arrived, Frank was already dead. You killed him. After finding the body, Vinnie moved Frank to the bed, staged it as 'sleeping', then set the fire. He thought this would protect you.
No... no...
是...是我做的...我用烛台砸了他...他当场就倒下去...头上流了好多血...
Yes... it was me... I hit him with the candlestick... he collapsed right there... so much blood from his head...
Vinnie他...他为我做了这么多...
Vinnie he... he did so much for me...
案件到此结束。Leonard Russo，你因涉嫌谋杀Frank Kowalski被逮捕。
The case ends here. Leonard Russo, you are under arrest on suspicion of murdering Frank Kowalski.
Emma，Foster那边有什么消息吗？
Emma, any news from Foster?
我联系了Foster，她说尸检报告还没完成，但初步骨骼分析显示可能是男性。（占位）
I contacted Foster. She says the autopsy report isn't finished yet, but the preliminary skeletal analysis suggests it may be male. (placeholder)

房间里还有其他人。
哦——对不起先生！我...我是 Rosa，Rosa Martinez。就是...就是做清洁的。
你在这里做什么。
我...月底了，Webb 先生要求彻底清洁。我一直在楼下走廊那边打扫...然后...然后听到了枪声。
然后我就上来了，大家都上来了，就是...就是她。[手指飞快地朝 Vivian 的方向比了一下，随即缩回去，低下头] 就是那把枪。她手里的那把枪。她一定是刚刚——
...一定是她杀的，先生。她手里还拿着枪呢。
慢慢说。
我...我说完了，先生。就是这样。
不是。你刚才抬头看我之前，眼睛先往那边瞟了一下。不是 Vivian 那个方向。
没...没有，先生。我就是在做清洁。
你说她杀了人。

Parameters.ParameterStr: 23:30 那一声——你在哪儿听到的？

Words: 你说 Vivian 杀了 Webb。你亲眼看见她开枪了？
我...我是说...她手里有枪。那把枪。Webb 先生就死在那里。然后...
谁手里拿着枪，就是谁做的，先生。这...这是明摆着的事。
她此刻手里正拿着那把枪。她一定是刚刚杀了 Webb 先生。
你没有回答我的问题。
先生...我只知道我看到的。
那声枪响。23:30——你在哪里？
楼下走廊...还是楼下，先生。打扫。就是...就是那一声。
那一声之前呢？还有别的动静吗？
没有。就只有 23:30 那一声。那之前楼上都是安静的...就...就只有那一声，先生。
而且...那个时候。我们...就是大家都上来了。那个时候里面只有她，就只有 Vivian 在里面。
23:30 枪响，当晚没有别的枪声。那个时候只有 Vivian 在里面。所以...所以只能是她。
你说话听起来，像是事先想好了要说这几句。
我...我就是说了我知道的事，先生。
好。
我...我可以继续去打扫了吗，先生。月底的清洁还没——
不行。你是目击者。
Rosa 说这是一场开枪之后的现场。但她不敢看这个房间里的任何人的眼睛。

蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 然后我们去查 Webb 这个人。[停顿一拍] 你想想——Rosa 的说法是"当时只有 Vivian 在场"，Morrison 把这个当作时间线的锚点。但 Rosa 为什么要在那个关键时间节点撒一个谎？一个人在说谎的时候，如果不是记错了，那就是她有一个需要保护的说法。这说法对谁有利？

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 不知道。现在还看不出来。

蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 所以要先把 Webb 的底翻清楚。[她转向大厅深处，声音放低了一点] Webb 昨晚雇了你——他预感到有危险，但他怀疑的那个人，他没有告诉你名字。一个人在觉得自己快被害死的时候，还是立了一份委托协议，还是把 Vivian 的继承权写进去——他在保护什么？他又在害怕谁？

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 对。每一个今晚在场的人，都和 Webb 有某种关系。我们逐个问，从 Webb 这个人是什么人问起。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 那就从 Webb 的委托协议书说起。你刚才说"他在保护什么"——你是有什么判断了？

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 问题不是他在保护 Vivian——那份合同已经写了。问题是他为什么要把"继承遗产"这条写进一份保护协议里。[她的笔尖指着本子上某一行]

Parameters.ParameterStr: 那份合同你仔细看过没有——除了保护 Vivian，还有什么特别的？
他惹上的人已经死了，还谈继承什么遗产。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: ……看过。"确保 Vivian 在 Webb 死后顺利继承遗产。"就这一条格外不像保护合同。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 对。"死后"——不是"万一有意外"，不是"如果他出了什么事"。是"死后"。这个词是确定的，预设的。他在立这份合同的时候，心里已经把自己的死当成一个必然事项在安排了。[她顿了一下] 这不是一个只是有点担心的人会写出来的措辞。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 所以他知道对方是谁。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 他现在已经死了。继承遗产这条不管是什么，现在优先级是搞清楚他为什么会死。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 这两件事是同一件事，Mr. Brennan。[她把本子翻到那一页，指着其中一行] "在 Webb 死后顺利继承遗产"——他立合同的时候心里已经把自己的死当成一个确定会来的事在安排了。他知道对方是谁，他预感那个人会赢，但他还是给 Vivian 留了退路。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 他怀疑的那个人，就在今晚。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 所以我们问 Webb 的事——是为了找到那个他怀疑的人。不是为了替他擦干净什么，是为了弄清楚他的处境，弄清楚他身边到底有什么人。[停顿] 从这里开始，每个人我们都要问到。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 如果 Webb 预感到自己会死，他怀疑的那个人，一定就在这栋楼里。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 那现在从哪里开始？

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 你来定顺序。我负责问话，你负责记住他们说话时的样子。[走出两步，侧头看他一眼，嘴角有一点什么压在里面没出来] 还有——Mr. Brennan，你那颗糖。

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 怎么了？

Location: 蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
蓝月亮酒吧大门入口 → 一楼大厅
Words: 今天我们去采访每个人的时候，能不能不要叼着。你就是这种打扮，他们会以为你来要债的。
行了。[语气很平，不像是感叹，更像是在确认一个事实] Tommy 那边打完了——把你听到的，从头说一遍。
酒吧不只做私酒。勒索生意存在，Tommy 以前是执行人——他亲手记的客户名单。11月中旬之后，他就不再执行了。
所以差额出来了。Webb 一个月 $15,000，酒吧账面 $4,000——中间那 $11,000，是从勒索来的。Tommy 一直知道，一直做账，但他说"现在不做了"。[她停笔，抬起眼] 他有没有解释为什么不做了？
说是 Webb 沉迷那个叫 Rita 的女人以后，就停止了勒索业务。从 11 月中旬开始的事。
Rita 出现，勒索停了，Tommy 退出——这三件事在同一个时间节点上。[她看着自己画的线，没有立刻往下说] 11 月中旬之后，勒索业务到底有没有继续，还是彻底停了？
Tommy 说他不参与了。但他没有说业务停了。他说的是"执行人换了，不是我"。
"不是我"——不是"没有人了"。[停顿，语气比刚才多了一点分量] 所以勒索的事还在继续，只是换了人。是谁，他说了吗？
没说。他没有点名字。
那就是他不确定，或者他确定但不敢说。[她直起腰，语气切换，像是在列重点] 这就是下一步的方向之一——接手勒索生意的人是谁？
Webb 是做勒索生意的，但谁是现在的执行人——这才是关键。执行人现在还在这栋楼里吗？他和 Webb 的死有没有关系？
Tommy 还说了一件事。
他说 Webb 惹上了一个大人物。说 Webb 想转移财产，准备跑路——他认为 Webb 是因为勒索惹上了什么不该惹的人。
大人物。[她重复了这两个字，像是在掂量它的重量，没有立刻评价]
他没有给名字。我看他那个神情——他心里是有猜测的。但他死活没有开口。
我知道。[停顿] Tommy 说到那两个字的时候，眼神往下飘了一下——不是在想，是在刻意不说。他有一个名字在嘴边，但他选择咽回去了。
他怕说出来。
一个做了多年勒索生意的账房，见过什么样的人的脸他心里有数。他把那个名字咽回去，不是因为他不确定——是因为他清楚那个名字说出来意味着什么。[停顿，拿起本子，把笔收好] 这不是一个普通的人。
Webb 的勒索生意——惹上了这种人，然后他死了。那个执行人换掉的时间节点，和 Webb 出事是同一个月。
我注意到了。但眼下这只是两件事在同一个时间发生了——我们还没有东西把它们连起来。[她抬起头，语气切换，重新清醒] 先把我们手里有的东西确认好，再往下推。
那下一步怎么走？

Parameters.ParameterStr: Vivian 那边——她说"要再想想"，那句话是什么意思？
Rosa 的时间线——23:30 枪声从来没有被反驳过，现在往哪里找？

Words: Vivian 听到委托协议书的事之后，说了一句"要再想想"。现在那边什么情况？
她反锁了工作室。[语气平，没有戏剧化] 这不一定是坏事——"要再想想"说明她以前的话不是全部的真相。她在调整。
她在认罪。调整不了什么。
一个真的杀了人的人，不会因为被告知"他其实想保护你"而动摇。她动摇了，说明她咬定自己杀了人这件事，本来就不是真的——或者至少不是完整的真相。[停顿] 现在她需要时间。我们不去打扰她。
23:30 那个时间线，我在 loop1 就没有东西反驳。现在有了什么吗？
还没有。[直接说] 但是我们现在多了一条信息——Tommy 说枪声来自酒吧南方，厨房和会客室方向。这是他亲耳听到的方向，和 Rosa 的说法能对上。
方向对上了，还是反驳不了时间。
对。方向对得上，但时间没有矛盾。[停顿] 但我们还没有重新检查过案发现场——Morrison 把那里封了，我们 loop1 结束前没有机会仔细找。现场还可能有我们漏掉的东西。
Rosa 的时间线说法一直没能反驳。下一步，我们要重新去检查案发现场——也许那里还有什么我们没看到的。
案发现场。[重复了这三个字，语气是确认，不是疑问]
Morrison 的人还守着，但案发现场那个房间——封锁期间只要有正当理由，我的记者证可以说得通。[停顿，语气一贯的直接] 我们可以再进去。
然后呢？假设里面有我们漏掉的东西——那东西能说明什么？
说明 23:30 那个时间节点可能不是案发的真实时间。如果不是——Rosa 的时间线说法就彻底站不住了。[她停了一下，语气没有变热，但更清楚了] 现在这只是一个可能。要变成证据，得进那个房间。
行。
今晚 Tommy 那边确认了：勒索生意存在，执行人换过人，Webb 在出事前就已经感觉到危险了。[她说话的时候笔已经在本子上动了] 这是我们拿到手的东西，不够，但比昨晚多了。
不够在哪里？
在于我们知道 Webb 惹上了什么人，但不知道是谁。我们知道执行人换了，但不知道换了谁。我们知道 Rosa 的时间线有问题，但手里没有东西能反驳它。[她停住脚步，转头看他，语气比刚才多了一点什么，不是笑，但是接近于那个方向] 不过好消息是，下一步去哪里，我们现在说清楚了。
案发现场。[停顿一拍，像是忽然想起来什么，侧头看他一眼] 还有——Mr. Brennan，你今天进 Webb 的办公室的时候，你是不是又在叼糖？
厨房还开着。
角落里有个人在切菜——背对着门，刀声很稳，节奏均匀，像是什么都没有发生过。
年轻，穿着厨师服，衣服洗得很干净。看起来比任何人都要镇定——只是刀停了一下，停了有大约一秒，然后继续。
你好。我是 Zack Brennan，今晚 Webb 先生雇来的。
先生。[点了点头，声音很轻，英语说得不算流利，字和字之间有轻微的停顿] 我知道你。Webb 先生……提起过。
那你就知道今晚出事了。
是。[低着头，声音没有波动，但有什么东西沉在声音下面] 很……抱歉。
你是这里的厨师？
兼职。[停了一停] 这里的兼职主厨。
在这之前，或者同时——还在哪里做事？
到处。[语气中性，没有特别躲闪，但也没有展开的意思] 打零工。修东西、送货——什么都做。这座城市……能做的事，我都做。
那是你的工资单？
是。这个月的。
就这点数字，很难过好日子。
Aye。[低声应了一句，像是一个习惯性的口气词，没有解释] 不够的。所以才……副业很多。
兼职主厨，加上送货和修东西。
你缺钱——是有什么要紧的用处？
家里。[就说了这两个字，然后又加了一句，语气很平] 家里需要用钱。医院不便宜。

普通的事。[停了停，补充] 送东西、取东西……有时候帮人修水管，修锁。这座城市里，有很多人需要人帮忙做……不贵的活儿。

厨房。[没有犹豫，但说的太快了一点] 全晚都在厨房里。[停了一拍，又补了一句，像是在解释] 帮厨 Tom 可以证明。

Parameters.ParameterStr: 你的副业——具体都在做什么？
你说你全晚都在厨房——能再说一遍吗？

Words: Zack 推开办公室的门。Tommy 正低头对着账本，笔尖停在某一行数字上，听到动静后抬头，眼镜片后的眼睛先是一紧，随即挤出一个体面的笑。

"Brennan 先生...您来了。我能...帮上什么忙？"
他站起来，手指在桌沿轻轻叩了一下，然后意识到这个动作，把手收回来放在身后。
"Webb 先生刚出事，你管账的，酒吧有什么生意你比谁都清楚。我想问几个问题。"

"当然，当然...Brennan 先生尽管问，我能帮到什么，一定说。"
他重新坐下来，双手交叠放在桌面上，姿态合作，眼神却在打量着 Zack 拿了什么没有。
"酒吧在做什么生意？"
"私酒。"他轻笑了一声，听起来不紧张，反倒有几分了然。"Brennan 先生，这一行谁都知道，不算秘密了。禁酒令这些年，芝加哥哪家馆子不沾？我们算规矩的。"
"规矩的？"

"对...就是私酒。其他的没有。"
他顿了一下，手指又在桌面叩了一下，随即停住。
"Webb 先生把酒吧开得稳当，从来不惹事。我管账，账本随时都在，干干净净的。"
"只做私酒？"

keyInfoType: statement
内容：Tommy——酒吧里只做了私酒生意，没有干别的（⚠谎言1）
